inscription.head:
No. 1.
(A.R. No. 92 of 1946.)
Narasānāyakanipalle, Venkatagiri Taluk, Nellore District.
inscription.p: Stone found near tank-bund of the village.

Damaged. Records the gift of dry and wet lands to the Brāhmaṇas of the village. It
also mentions two persons viz., Cheṁnappa and Dorappa. Other details are lost. In characters
of the 12th-13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 2.
(A.R. No. 93 of 1946.)
Veṁbulūru, Venkatagiri Taluk, Nellore District.
inscription.p: Stone erected in a field to the east of the village.
inscription.h4: This is dated Virōdhikṛit, Mārgaśira ba. 5.
inscription.p:
Damaged. States that Koṇḍa Nāraśiṁharāju made some repairs to the tank called
Siddhirāju-Cheruvu, for his own merit, with the consent of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kōṭipi
[Raṅga]rājayya.

edition.p: The rest of the lines are damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 3.
(A.R. No. 94 of 1946.)
Inuguṇta, Venkatagiri Taluk, Nellore District.
inscription.p: Stone found in the village.

This is dated Śaka 1524, Śubhakṛit, Jyēshṭha śu. 14, corresponding to 1602 A.D. May
24, Monday.

It records the gift of lands by Śrimanmahāmaṇḍalēśvara Gobbūri Jaggarājayya for
maintance of the tank at Inuguṇta for its maintenance, for the merit of Obarājayya Tirumala.

l.te: rāja rājaparamēśvara vīrapratāpa śrī vīrabhujabala vīraveṁka-

inscription.head:
No. 4.
(A.R. No. 95 of 1946.)
Vendōḍu, Gudur Taluk, Nellore District.
inscription.p: Stone set up in the compound of the Vendōḍu-Amma temple.
inscription.h4: This is dated Īśvara, Mārgaśira ba. 13.
inscription.p:
It records the gift of lands and certain incomes to the shrines of Vendōṭamma and
Mallikārjuna, by Obuḷu Reḍḍi, son of Koṇḍāpuram Vāba Reḍḍi, who was the agent of Nāgā
Reḍḍi. In characters of the 16th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 5.
(A.R. No. 111 of 1946.)
Agaḍūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab set up infront of the Chennakēśava temple.

This is dated Śaka 1466, Krōdhi, Kārttika, śu. 12, corresponding to 1544 A.D.,
October 27, Monday.

Records the gift of local toll income collected from markets (Pēṁṭastaḷasuṁkhamu) for
conducting the various festivals such as the floating festivals on daśami day and Uṭla-tirunāla
to the god Chennakēśava at Amaḍūru by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Raṅgayadēva-Mahārāja, son of
Saṁmaka Tipayadēva of the Ātrēya-gōtra, for the merit of his parents and Yeṟaguḍi
Ti[mma]ma Rājayya.

inscription.head:
No. 6.
(A.R. No. 112 of 1946.)
Agaḍūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Three sides of a mutilated slab lying near the Chennakēśava temple.

Much damaged. Refers to a gift made in the presence of the god Viṭhala on the
banks of the Tuṅgabhadra at Vijayanagara on the occasion of a lunar eclipse. Other details
are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 7.
(A.R. No. 113 of 1946.)
Alavalapāḍu, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Śiva temple in the village.
inscription.h4: This is dated Ānanda, Mārgaśira ba. 8.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to record some gift to the potters by the village officials viz., Reḍḍis
and Karaṇams. In characters of about the 17th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 8.
(A.R. No. 114 of 1946.)
Alavalapāḍu, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab set up at the entrance of the Chennakēśava temple.

This is dated Śaka 1705, Śōbhakṛit, Vaiśākha śu. 10. corresponding to 1782 A.D.
April 23, Tuesday.

Records that Timmakka, wife of Narasāreḍḍi, son of Yidāreḍḍi of Gaukanapalle and
daughter of Koṇḍāreḍḍi of Alavalapāḍu, renovated the temples of the gods Chennakēśava and
Sāṁba (Śiva) and restored the daily offerings.

inscription.head:
No. 9.
(A.R. No. 115 of 1946.)
Alavalapāḍu, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab set up at the entrance of the Chennakēśava temple.

This is dated Śaka 1473, Virōdhikṛit, Pushya ba. 30, Monday, Ardhōdaya, corresponding
to 1552 A.D., January 25, Monday.

Records the gift of dues to the Dommaris from the temple of Bhairavēśvara at
Mōpūri located in the Gaṇḍikōṭa-sīma in favour of the temple of Bhōgēśvara and Chennakēśvara
of Aluvalapāḍu by Kāki Pedarāju, son of Abbarāju for the merit of the twenty-four families
of Dommaras.

l.te: kiṁni śrī ceṁnnakēśvara dēvunikini doṁmari ya-
taka vēnani kāki ·abbarāju koḍuku pedarāju savi-
ghaṁḍḍi kōṭasīmalōnu mōpūri bhairavēśvara dēvuni celyē ·aluvala-

inscription.head:
No. 10.
(A.R. No. 116 of 1946.)
Chāgalēru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab built into the platform of the Śiva temple outside the village.
This inscription reads from the bottom to the top.

Seems to record the death of one Peddini in a cattle raid. In characters of about the
8th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 11.
(A.R. No. 117 of 1946.)
Chāgalēru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Stone lying near the Āñjanēya temple outside the village.

Wornout. Seems to record a gift of land to the learned Brāhmaṇas of the village. In
characters of about the 16th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 12.
(A.R. No. 118 of 1946.)
Chāgalēru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Another stone lying near the Āñjanēya temple outside the village.
inscription.h4: This is dated Śaka year 1. 88 Piṅgaḷa, Phālguṇa śu. 10.

inscription.head:
No. 13.
(A.R. No. 119 of 1946.)
Chāgalēru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab lying near the village.
inscription.h4: This is dated Dhātu, Jyēsṭha śu. 5.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Seems to record the gift of income from the marriage tax for maintaining
a lamp to god Hanumān. In characters of about the 16th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 14.
(A.R. No. 120 of 1946.)
Cherlōpalle (hamlet of Santakōvūru), Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the dilapidated site of the Āñjanēya temple.

This is dated in Śaka 1459, Hēmaḷaṁbi, Śrāvaṇa ba. 1, Saturday, corresponding to
1537 A.D., July 7, Saturday, f.d.t. °21 (ba. is a mistake for śu.).

Records a gift of land for daily offerings to the god Chennakēśava at Mārakapuram,
a suburb of Cherlōpalle, by Timmināyaka, son of Timmināyaka of Penugoṇḍa. The ceded
village is stated to have been bestowed as amara fief to the donor by Peda-Tirumalarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 15.
(A.R. No. 121 of 1946.)
Gollala Gūḍūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Broken slab lying near the village.
Very much damaged. Seems to mention Viraṁma.

inscription.head:
No. 16.
(A.R. No. 122 of 1946.)
Gollala Gūḍūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Another broken slab lying near the village.
This is dated Pramādi, Bhādrapada śu. 12.
The record is badly damaged. It mentions cyclic year and other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 17.
(A.R. No. 123 of 1946.)
Gondipalle, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Stone lying in a field about half a mile from the village.
Damaged. In archaic characters of about the 8th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 18.
(A.R. No. 124 of 1946.)
Inagalūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the dilapidated Chennakēśava temple.

This is dated Śaka 1469, Plavaṅga, Bhādrapada śu. 12. corresponding to 1547 A.D.,
August 27, Saturday.

Damaged. Seems to record a gift by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmayadēva-Mahārāja and
Yaṟaguḍi Tirumaladēva Mahārāja to the god Chennakēśava, who was worshipped by the
barber (Mangaḷa) community.

inscription.head:
No. 19.
(A.R. No. 125 of 1946.)
Mōpūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Top of an image (male) kept in the compound of the Bhairavēśvara temple.
Reads “Baiyana Vīruḍu”. In characters of about the 15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 20.
(A.R. No. 126 of 1946.)
Mōpūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Top of another (male) image in the same place.
Reads “Madhukaya Vīruṁḍu”. In characters of about the 15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 21.
(A.R. No. 127 of 1946.)
Mōpūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Top of another (male) image in the same place.
Reads “Pōtaya Vīruḍu”. In characters of about the 15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 22.
(A.R. No. 128 of 1946.)
Mōpūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Top of another (male) image in the same place.
Reads “China Maṁchaya Vīruṁḍu”. In characters of about the 15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 23.
(A.R. No. 129 of 1946.)
Mōpūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Top of another (male) image in the same place.
Reads “Peda Maṁchaya Vīruḍu”. In characters of about the 15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 24.
(A.R. No. 130 of 1946.)
Mōpūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Top of another (male) image in the same temple.
Mentions god Bhairava of Mōpuri. In characters of about the 15th century A.D.,

inscription.head:
No. 25.
(A.R. No. 131 of 1946.)
Mōpūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Top of another (male) image in the same place.
States that Aṁgajāle Rājanāyiḍu installed the image of the hero Immaḍi Sidōya.

inscription.head:
No. 26.
(A.R. No. 132 of 1946.)
Mutukūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Inner side of a door post of the Chennakēśava shrine.
Only the imprecatory portion is preserved. In characters of about the 16th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 27.
(A.R. No. 133 of 1946.)
Nāgūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Āñjanēya temple outside the village.

This is dated Śaka 1472, Phālguṇa śu. 15, Friday corresponding to 1551 A.D. February
20, Friday, °80. The cyclic year was Virōdhikṛit. Śaka 1472 appears to be a mistake for 1473.

Fragmentary. Seems to record a gift of land for the worship of the god Chennakēśava
at Nāgūru by Pedda Timmana and China Timmana. Mentions Etirāja Ōbaḷayadēva Mahārāja.

l.te: tsara pālguṇa śu 15 śu śrīman ma • • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 28.
(A.R. No. 134 of 1946.)
Nāgūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Āñjanēya temple outside the village.

Damaged and fragmentary. Seems to record a gift of land to [Bhō]laśakti-Paṇḍita for
the satram of the god Bhōgēśvara. In characters of about the 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 29.
(A.R. No. 135 of 1946.)
Nallacheruvupalle, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab set up on the road side in the village.
This is dated Durmati, Chaitra śu. 8.

Damaged. Seems to record some concessions in land tenure to the cultivators by
Mahānāyaṅkācharya Gōpatirāja.

inscription.head:
No. 30.
(A.R. No. 136 of 1946.)
Nallacheruvupalle, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near a well inside in the village.

This is dated Śaka 1453, Khara, Kārttika śu. 15, corresponding to 1531 A.D. October
25, Wednesday.

The inscription states that the king Achyutarāya gifted a land in the village Balapanūru
as an agrahāra on Sarvamānya tenure to one Basavadīkshitulayya. Now certain measure of
land in this agrahāra has been gifted to the god Bhairavēśvara of Mōpūru by Parivalūri
Virusayya on the orders of his master Basavadīkshitulayya.

inscription.head:
No. 31.
(A.R. No. 137 of 1946.)
Nandipalle, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab lying near Nandibāvi about half a mile away from the village.

This is dated in Śaka 1438, Dhātu, Adhika-Śrāvaṇa 15. Corresponding to 1516 A.D.
Two dates July 13 Sunday and July 29 Tuesday as the week day not given.

Records a gift of land by Gōvindayya, son of Nāchirājayya and Śaṅku Mallayya, son
of Sūlada Mallikhārjuna Oḍeya. The land is stated to have been granted to the donors by the
king.

inscription.head:
No. 32.
(A.R. No. 138 of 1946.)
Peddajūṭūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab built into the platform of the dhvajastaṁbha in the Śiva temple.
inscription.h4: This is dated Nandana, Jyēshṭha śu. 12.
inscription.p:
Records the setting up of the dhvajastaṁbha in the Īśvara temple at Mallelakuṁṭa by
Karaṇaṁ Veṅkaṭarāvu, son of Chinna Subbarāo of Peddajūṭūru. Further, it mentions Chinnappōḍu
and Venkaṭanna, who cast the dhvajastaṁbha.

inscription.head:
No. 33.
(A.R. No. 139 of 1946.)
Peddajūṭūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Śiva temple.

Śaka 14[51 ?], Virōdhi, [Kārttika] śu. 15, Monday, probably A.D. 1529 October 18,
Monday, śu. 15 had ended at °25 the previous day.

Damaged. Records the construction of the temple of the god Nāganātha and provision
made for the worship therein by Kāḍama Nāyaka, son of Sōmināyani Rāmaya.

inscription.head:
No. 34.
(A.R. No. 140 of 1946.)
Peddajūṭūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Two slabs set up in the Vēṇugōpalasvāmin temple.

This is dated Śaka 143[0], Vibhava, Kārttika śu. 15, corresponding to 1508 A.D.,
December, 7 Thursday.

Records the gift of some lands to the god Nāganātha for daily worship and offerings
by Raṅganātha, son of Jāvunayāka.

inscription.head:
No. 35.
(A.R. No. 141 of 1946.)
Peddajūṭūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Stone lying in a field known as Chinna Veṅkaṭādri-chēnu.

States that the field lying on the boundaries of the villages Peda-Jūṭūru and China-
Jūṭūru is a gift to a brāhmaṇa and a field to the engraver of the inscription, viz. Patevatōju.
In characters of about the 16th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 36.
(A.R. No. 142 of 1946.)
Pulivendla, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the shrine of the goddess in the Raṅganāthasvāmin temple.

Seems to record the gift of some lands to the goddess and contains an imprecatory
verse. In late characters.

inscription.head:
No. 37.
(A.R. No. 143 of 1946.)
Pulivendla, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab lying near the Nāni-Kāluva about a mile away from the village.

This is dated Śaka 1296, Ānanda, Kārttika śu. 1, Vaḍḍavāra, corresponding to 1374
A.D. October 7, Saturday, °25.

Records the gift of land in the village Pulivendla on daśavandha tenure to Perumāḍireḍḍi
by the chief Bhairavadēvarāja (who had titles Pāṇḍyarāyagajakēsari and
Kalukaḍapurvarādhīśvara) of Pulivendla. It is also stated that the land was situated near the
canal, to the west of the village, dug by him.

l.te: vvatsara kā¨rttika śu 1 va svasti śrī maṁnma-

inscription.head:
No. 38.
(A.R. No. 144 of 1946.)
Rāmireddipalle, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the field of Nallagāri Yallareḍḍi.

States that the field is a gift to the god Bhairava of Mrōpuru by Nāyili Nāgināyya of
Nāgūru for the merit of Kaṁparāja and Maṁgadēvi-amma. In characters of the 14th century
A.D.,

inscription.head:
No. 39.
(A.R. No. 145 of 1946.)
Santakōvūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Pillar set up in front of the Āñjanēya temple.

This is dated Śaka 1454, Nandana, Māgha śu 11, Wednesday corresponding to 1533
A.D., February 5, Wednesday °75.

Records a gift of toll-income, marriage tax (Peṇḍlisuṅkamu) and land to the gods
Chennakēśvara and Hanumān of Kōvūru in Poṭladoriti-sima for celebrating the festivals on
the occasion of Bhīshma-ēkādaśi, for the merit of the king by Rāmānāyaka, son of
Pāpināyaka, grandson of Rāmānāyaka and great grandson of Aubhaḷa-nāyaka, who held the
village as the amara fief. The village was under the administration of Salkarāja Peda-
Tirumalarāja, who received the Chennuri-poṭladurti-sīma from Achyutarāya as his fiefdom.

inscription.head:
No. 40.
(A.R. No. 146 of 1946.)
Santakōvūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab lying in a field about a mile away on the boundry of the village.

Refers to a gift of paddy fields (pannasa) by one Dhavaliyarāja, probably of the
Mahābali family. Refers also to the coin gadyāna and a land measure maruturu.

inscription.head:
No. 41.
(A.R. No. 147 of 1946.)
Velamvāripalle, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab set up in a garden called Sāsavrāja Guṇḍalu about a mile away from the village.
This is dated Dundubhi, Pushya śu. 10, Friday, Makara Saṁkrānti.

Records a gift of 10 tūmus of land by Mahānāyakāchārya Paḷi-Nāyaka, son of Brammi-
nāyaka for the merit of his parents on the occasion of Makarasaṅkrānti.

inscription.head:
No. 42.
(A.R. No. 148 of 1946.)
Vēmula, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: On a slab setup in this village.

This is dated Śaka 1603. Durmati, Māgha śu 5, Wednesday, corresponding to 1682
A.D., January 4. Wednesday 10.

Damaged. It records the gift of some lands in the village Vēmula by Veṅkaṭādri
Timmarasa of Amālimōpalli, while one Maharāyarāja Śrī-Mauroji Aṁto Tirvudhāru
mahātātavoyatu was ruling Pulivendla (?).

l.te: śrī svasti śrī jayābhyudaya śālivāhana śa-
nāma saṁvvatsara māgha śu 5 bu mahārājādhirāja śrī
• • • śilāśāsanaṁ yaṁdu brāhmaṇa kṣatriya vaiśya śūdra lōya • •

inscription.head:
No. 43.
(A.R. No. 149 of 1946.)
Ūḍavagaṇḍla, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Two fragments of a broken pillar set up at two different places inside the village.

Damaged. Seems to records a gift of 50 maṟutus of land (pannasa) to Uḍugugaṇdlya and
to Paḷḷa Pishṭamayya of Alikoṇḍala by Bejayitāyya. Refers to Rattaguḍis. In characters of
about the 9th century A.D.

edition.h4: First side of the first piece.
First side of the second piece.
Second side of the first piece.
Second side of the second piece.

inscription.head:
No. 44.
(A.R. No. 150 of 1946.)
Eṟṟabaḷḷa, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab lying outside the Pañchaliṅga cave.
inscription.h4: This is dated [Śaka 1363], Durmati, Pushya ba ...
inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to register a gift of land to the god Pañchaliṅga-siddhayya made into
the hands of Nāgadēvayya, son of Jaṅgama Saṅgamanātha, for the merit of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Vallabhaya and his son Timmayya.

l.te: • • • la me • • nu kotula guḍaṁnni duma • •

inscription.head:
No. 45.
(A.R. No. 151 of 1946.)
Eṟṟabaḷḷa, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Brow of a cliff outside the Pañchaliṅga cave facing the west.

This is dated Śaka 1364, Dundubhi, Kārttika śu. 15, Krittika nakshatra corresponding
to 1442 A.D., October 18, Thursday, f.d.t. °69.

Written in chaste Telugu prose, the inscription is eulogy in nature, while the gift
portion is vague. It refers to Prauḍha Dēvarāya, son of Vijayarāya, whose feet were
worshipped (by his feudatories and officers) and who was Dēvendra on earth. His subordinate,
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Timmabhūpa, [son of] Vallabhēndra of Ātrēya-gōtra and Sōma-kula, was
described as having court which consisted of many wise men. He bears the title Erigola-
surabāṇa. Then Pulivendla is stated to have been situated south of Śrīparvata. The Pancha-
lingēśvara-kshētra is said to lie on the hill Chanukoṇḍa, on the river Pālēru and north of
Guṇḍālaya.

l.te:
śubhamastu namastuṁga śiraścuṁbi caṁdracāmara cāravētrailokya nagarāraṁbha mūlastaṁbhāya
śaṁbhavē | śrīmadrājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara ·aṣṭadigrā • •

ja manōbhayaṁkara śrī vīrapratāpa vijayarāya mahārāja kumāruṁḍaina prauḍhadēvēṁdruṁḍani

inscription.head:
No. 46.
(A.R. No. 152 of 1946.)
Eṟṟabaḷḷa, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Rock inside the Pañchaliṅga cave facing the east.

This is dated [Śaka 1363], Durmati, Pushya śu. 15, Thursday. corresponding to 1441
A.D., December 28, Thursday, śu. 15 had ended at °97 the previous day.

Damaged. Records a gift of lands at Eṟambaḍi and Pulivindla for the daily offerings
and conducting 16 kinds of rituals of homages (Shoḍaśōpachāra) to the god Pañchaliṅgala-
siddhayya on the Amṛitagiri by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Timmayya, son of Vallabhayya. The gift
was made into the hands of Nāgadēva, son of Jaṅgama Saṁgamadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 47.
(A.R. No. 153 of 1946.)
Kopparti, Cuddapah Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Two slabs lying near the Āñjānēya temple.

This is dated Śaka 1488, Parābhava, Vaiśākha śu. 15. corresponding to 1567 A.D.,
April 15, Wednesday.

Damaged. Seems to record the repairs to the Rāmataṭāka (tank) at Kṛishṇarāyapura-
agrahāra near Kopparti and the construction of a sluice to it. The name of the person who
repaired is not clear but in the ancestry, the names of Garidiju Bōkarāju and Gaṅgarāju are
mentioned, who seem to be the grandfather and father respectively of the person. It is also
said that this was dedicated to Alamelumaṅga and Veṅkatēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 48.
(A.R. No. 154 of 1946.)
Pedda Pasupula, Jammalamadugu Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab built into the side wall of the Kōnēru.

This is dated Śaka 1655, Paridhāvi, Āśvija śu. 2. Bhānuvāra, corresponding to 1732
A.D., September 10, Sunday 30.

Records the construction of the eastern gate in the fort wall of Peddapasapula by
Yusupu Khān (Yusuf khān) son of Hajarāti Nawab, Jamshed Khān.

l.te: bha | mūlastaṁbhāya śaṁbhave || svasti śrī jayābhyudaya śālivā-
¨rkasthāyigā vrāyiṁcci veśina śilāśāsanamu śrī śrī śrī śrī je

inscription.head:
No. 49.
(A.R. No. 155 of 1946.)
Pedda Pasupula, Jammalamadugu Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab built into the side wall of the Kōnēru.

This is dated Śaka 1675, Śrīmukha, Āshāḍha śu. 5. Śukravāra corresponding to 1753
A.D., July 6, Friday śu. 5 had ended at °51 on the previous day.

Records the construction of the south-western tower of the fort at Pasapula by
Hajarāti Abdul Gani Khān, son of Nawab Abdul Khādar Khān.

l.te: lavāru paragaṇe pasapula grāmānaku kōṭaku nayi-
riti mūlana kaṭiṁccina buruju śilā śāsanaṁ || śrī

inscription.head:
No. 50.
(A.R. No. 158 of 1946.)
Mācherla, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District.
inscription.p: Grey pillar in a field belonging to Pillabōyani Nāgayya, in the village.
Fragmentary. In characters of about the 8th century A.D.
Damaged and unintelligible.

inscription.head:
No. 51.
(A.R. No. 1 of 1947.)
Ahōbaḷapuram, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab lying near the Ahōbaleśvara temple.
This is dated Rākshasa, Kārtika śu. 11.

Records the setting up of the balipīṭha to the god Ahōbaleśvara by Kannaya, son
of Sāraṁ Nāganna. In late characters.

l.te: rākṣasa saṁvatsara kā¨rtika śu 11 nāḍu sāra

inscription.head:
No. 52.
(A.R. No. 2 of 1947.)
Balapanūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Stone set up inside the compound of the Śiva temple.
Records the gift of pannasa of 100 maṟutulu

inscription.head:
No. 53.
(A.R. No. 3 of 1947.)
Balapanūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Stone built into a platform inside the compound of the Śiva temple.
This is dated Śaka 14[76], Ānanda, Chaitra [ba. 7], Thursday. Irregular.
Mentions Mutira Bhatimayya, son of Goparāju. Other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 54.
(A.R. No. 4 of 1947.)
Gurijāla, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Stone lying near the dilapidated temple of Vighneśvara.

This is dated Śaka 147[5] Pramādicha. Jyēshṭha śu. 10 corresponding to 1553 A.D.
May 22, Monday.

Fragmentary and damaged. Seems to record some gift made to two individuals (names
lost).

inscription.head:
No. 55.
(A.R. No. 5 of 1947.)
Gurijāla, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Stone set up near the dilapidated temple of Vighneśvara.
This is dated Paridhāvi, Kārttika ba. 2, Thursday.

Records contain concessions granted to farmers (Kāṁpu) of Gurijāla by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Tirumalarāja Veṅgaḷarājadēva. In characters of about the 16th century A.D.

l.te: pina pāpāna pōvuvāru || śrī śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 56.
(A.R. No. 6 of 1947.)
Gurijāla, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Stone set up in a field named Rātipaṁpu-chēnu near the Village.
This is dated Śaka • • • Ānanda, Jyēshṭha śu. 10.

Damaged and incomplete. Seems to record a gift of land by Basvayya, son of
Mallasāni Tippa, in the area that was given as uṁbali by Gobbūri Narasarāju. Mentions
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Tirumalarāju Veṅgalarāju.

inscription.head:
No. 57.
(A.R. No. 7 of 1947.)
Kasanūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab built into the southern side of the platform of the Chennakēśava temple.
This is dated Śaka 147[5] Pramādicha, Bhādrapada ba.

Damaged and incomplete. Seems to record a gift to god Chennakēśvara of Śivanūru
lias Tiruvēṁgaḷa-Nellūru by Sāḷuva Lakshmayya, Sāḷuva Bukkarāju, Sāḷuva Timayya, Kaṭhāri
Bairāju and some other members of the Dommari (acrobat) community, who offered their
obeisance to the deity.

l.te: śubhamastu svasti śrī jayābhyudaya śālivāhana śakavaru¨rṣaṁbu-
lu 1475 ·agunēṁṭi pramādica saṁvvatsara bhādrapada ba. śrī

inscription.head:
No. 58.
(A.R. No. 8 of 1947.)
Kasanūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Stone lying near a well in the village.

Damaged and fragmentary. Seems to gift some land. In characters of about the 16th
century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 59.
(A.R. No. 9 of 1947.)
Kasanūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Stone setup near a well in the village.

Damaged. Purport of the inscription is not clear. In characters of about the 15th century
A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 60.
(A.R. No. 10 of 1947.)
Kasanūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Stone lying by the side of the road leading to the market.
Mutilates. Mentions Aggaḷaya. In characters of about the 9th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 61.
(A.R. No. 11 of 1947.)
Kasanūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Stone setup near the Āñjanēya temple inside the village.

Seems to record a gift of 5 maṟuturs of land as Pannasa. In characters of about the
8th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 62.
(A.R. No. 11 of 1947.)
Kasanūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Stone setup near the Āñjanēya temple in the village.
This is dated Khara, Jyēshṭha śu. 15.

The record was written indifferently. Mentions Vuppa Nāgi Reddi, the headman of the
village Kasalūru and Nāpi Reddi, Tingarappa and Ānanttayya. The inscription was engraved
by Āpayya.

inscription.head:
No. 62a.
(A.R. No. 12 of 1947.)
Kasanūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Stone setup near the Āñjanēya temple of the village (below the above inscription).
This is dated Vijaya, Āshāda ba. 30, Friday.

States that the Reḍḍi residents Karaṇam of the village Tiruvengala Nellūru had
ordered the residents of village not to venture out of the village (?) on the new moon days.
In characters of about the 16th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 63.
(A.R. No. 13 of 1947.)
Lōmaḍa, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Stone lying in the ruins outside the village.
Damaged and fragmentary. In characters of about the 8th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 64.
(A.R. No. 14 of 1947.)
Lōmaḍa, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Another slab lying in the same place.
This is dated Parthiva, Kārttika śu. 12.
Seems to record a gift of land in Puliviṁdala by Mutyāla Timmayya.

inscription.head:
No. 65.
(A.R. No. 15 of 1947.)
Lōmaḍa, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Third slab lying in the same place.

This is dated Śaka 1488, Akshaya, Vaiśākha śu. 15 corresponding to 1566 A.D. June
2, Sunday.

Records a gift of land for burning a perpetual lamp to the god Kēśava at Lōmaḍa,
by Raṅganāthadēva Mahārāja, on the orders of his brother Maṇḍalēśvara Peda-Nārasiṁharājayya.
These two were the sons of Aubhaḷarāja and grandsons of Peda-Aubhaḷadēva-mahārāja, of the
Naṁdēla family belonging to the Ātrēya-gōtra, Āpastamba-sūtra and Yajuśākhā. The village
is stated to have been granted to the chief as Amaranāyaṁkaram by the king.

l.te: • ku paḍumara puliviṁdala raṁganādhanimaḍiki dakṣana kha maḍi 2

inscription.head:
No. 66.
(A.R. No. 16 of 1947.)
Lōmaḍa, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Stone setup in the ruins outside the village.

This is dated Śaka 1469, Plavaṅga, Mārgaśira ba. 10 corresponding to 1547 A.D.,
November 7, Monday.

Records that assignment of toll-income collected from the village Lōmaḍa, of Ghaṇḍikōṭa
sīma to Kṛishṇarāya-bhūshaṇam and Achyutarāyabhūshaṇam by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Naṁdēla
Timmayadēva at the bidding of Rāmārāja, on the command of the king Sadāśivarāya.

inscription.head:
No. 67.
(A.R. No. 17 of 1947.)
Paidipālem, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Stone near dilapidated temple in the village.
This is dated Śubhakrit.

Damaged and worn out. Seems to refer to some exemptions in taxes made to the
Dommari community.

inscription.head:
No. 68.
(A.R. No. 18 of 1947.)
Paidipālem, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Another Stone near a dilapidated temple in the village.

This is dated Śaka 1466, Krōdhi, Kārttika śu. 11, corresponding to 1544 A.D.,
October 26, Sunday.

It records the remission of the barbers tax and other levies such as Siddhāyam, Kāṇike,
Kaḍāyam Veṭṭivirāḷam and dommari-kāsu in Sidhimayi (Sidhanuyi in Gutti-durgam) by
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarājadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 69.
(A.R. No. 19 of 1947.)
Pulivendla, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Broken Stone lying outside the village.

Mutilated. Refers to the provinces (rājya) of Kaṁdanavōlu, Chandragiri and Rāyadurga.
In characters of about the 16th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 70.
(A.R. No. 20 of 1947.)
Pulivendla, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Piece of a stone near the Āñjanēya temple.

Fragmentary. Seems to record remission of marriage tax on certain settlements of the
Golla community in the Pulivindala-sīma by Narasarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 71.
(A.R. No. 21 of 1947.)
Rāvulakolanu, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Stone in a field known as āvula-doḍḍi-gaḍḍa.

This is dated Śaka 1476, Ānanda, Pushya śu 1 Sunday, Makara-Saṁkrānti corresponding
to 1554 A.D., January 3, however the day was Wednesday.

Records the construction of a canal in the name of his mother, Kāvēramma by
Narasanāyaka, son of Kasavināyaka, who was the mace-bearer of the Mahāmaṇḍaleśvara
Mēdinimisara-gaṁda Kaṭhāri-sāluva Immaḍi Narasiṁharāya Mahārāya.

inscription.head:
No. 72.
(A.R. No. 22 of 1947.)
Suṅkēsula, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Slab built into the platform called Chuḍammakaṭṭa.
This is dated Śaka • • • • • śukla, Jyēshṭha.

Records a gift of land to Nāgōja Timmayya for having constructed a well at
Suṅkēsula by Mahānāyakāchārya Ruju Bāpayya-Basavayya-Kētivayya-Nāgayya, who held the
village as fief from Sāḷuva Gōvindayya.

l.te: duku mīku mānyaṁgānu peṭṭina cenu vū[ri]-

inscription.head:
No. 73.
(A.R. No. 23 of 1947.)
Suṅkēsula, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Broken Stone lying near the village chāvaḍi.

Fragmentary. Seems to refers to a cattle raid. In characters of about the 9th century
A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 74.
(A.R. No. 24 of 1947.)
Suṅkēsula, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Stone lying near the well called Peda-bāvi outside the village.

Fragmentary. Contains only the imprecatory portion. In characters of about the 8th
century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 75.
(A.R. No. 25 of 1947.)
Toṇḍūru, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.
inscription.p: Stone lying in the dilapidated site of the Gōpālasvāmin temple outside the village.

This is dated Śaka 1483, Durmati Chaitra ba. 10 corresponding to 1561 A.D.,
April 9, Wednesday.
Damaged and worn out. Seems to refer to some gifts made by Timmarāju.

inscription.head:
No. 76.
(A.R. No. 80 of 1947.)
Tirukkaṇnamaṅgai, Mannargudi Taluk, Tanjore District, Tamil Nadu.
inscription.p: West and south Paṭṭi of the Bhaktavatsalaperumāḷ temple.
This is dated Subhānu, Chaitra śu. 15.

Records the gift of 15 1/2 vēlis of land to Lakshmaṇa-munulu for the expenses of
running a feeding in house Rāmānujakūṭam, for conducting worship and offerings and for
repairs to the temple of the god Bhaktavatsalaperumaḷ at Tirukaṇṇamaṅgai.

inscription.head:
No. 77.
(A.R. No. 145 of 1947.)
Śarapharājapuram, Dronachalam Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Rock near the Būrgamma-Kaṭṭa sluice on the way to Peṇḍēkallu.
In Chalukyan characters of about the 8th century A.D.
Reads: Sagumame.

inscription.head:
No. 78.
(A.R. No. 146 of 1947.)
Peṇḍēkallu, (hamlet of Sarapharājapuram), Dronachalam Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Nandi Pillar set up in front of the Īśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1598, Nala, Jyēshṭha ba. 30, Thursday, solar eclipse corresponding
to 1676 A.D. June 1, Thursday. Solar eclipse.

Registers a gift of land by the Reḍḍis (viz., Papireddi, Venkatreddi, Timmareddi and
Buchireddi) and Karaṇams (viz., Saravarāju and Mangalagiri) of the village of Sarapharājapuram
situated midway between Rāmaḷḷakōta and Pendēkallu to Vābaṁbhaṭlu, son of Narasambhaṭlu,
the archaka of the temple of Rāmeśvarasvāmin for conducting worship and offerings god.
The gift was made during the administration of the Kandanavōlu-Paṭṭana by Sarapharāju-
khānu, son of Bāḍēkhan Sāhebu.

edition.h4: continued at the bottom of the first side.

inscription.head:
No. 79.
(A.R. No. 147 of 1947.)
Gōpālapaṭnam, Sarvasiddhi Taluk, Visakhapatnam District.
inscription.p: On the threshold of the entrance into the shrine of Dharmaliṅgēśvara.
This is dated 30th year, Uttarāyaṇa.

Records a gift of [50] sheep for conducting perpetual lamp to the temple of Pattiśvara-
mahādeva.

inscription.head:
No. 80.
(A.R. No. 151 of 1947.)
Hyderabad, Rangareddy District.
inscription.p: Stone slab preserved in the Museum.
Mentions the village Oṟeūru, Kandūru and Bhōgavrōlu.

inscription.head:
No. 81.
(A.R. No. 1 of 1948.)
Amaḷḷadinne, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Stone set up near the village Chāvaḍi.

Seems to record a gift by the members of the Vipravinōdin community (details lost).
In characters of about the 16th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 82.
(A.R. No. 3 of 1948.)
Chikkēpalle (hamlet of Chāgallu), Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the temple of Pōlēramma.

This is dated Śaka 1720, Kālayukti, Śrāvaṇa ba. 12, Friday corresponding to 1798
A.D. September 7, Friday: 54 (The month was Nija-Śrāvaṇa).

Records the construction of the temple of Channakēśava by Nārāyaṇa-Reḍḍi, a
resident of Chikkepalle, a majara (hamlet) of Chāgallu.

inscription.head:
No. 83.
(A.R. No. 3A of 1948.)
Chikkēpalle, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Below the above inscription.
This is dated Ānanda.

States that, under the orders of Nummatānu Sāhēbu Nārāyana-Reḍḍi dug out a canal
near Chāgallu and assigned daśavandha land for its upkeep.

inscription.head:
No. 84.
(A.R. No. 4 of 1948.)
Chikkēpalle, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Below the above inscription.
This is dated Ānanda.

States that, under the orders of Nummatānu Sāhēbu Nārāyana-Reddi dug out a canal
near Chāgallu and assigned daśavandha land for its upkeep.

inscription.head:
No. 85.
(A.R. No. 7 of 1948.)
Chukkalūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Chennakēśava temple.

This is dated Śaka 1671, Prajōtpatti, Kārtika śu. 11 Wednesday. Irregular. The Cyclic
and Śaka years do not tally.

Damaged. Seems to record a gift of land to the god Chennakēśava at Chukalūru by
Majalā of Kāśyapa gōtra. Other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 86.
(A.R. No. 8 of 1948.)
Chukkalūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Stone lying near the Āñjanēya temple.

This is dated Śaka 1466, Krōdhi, Kārttika śu. 11 corresponding to 1544 A.D. October
26, Sunday °65.

Seems to record the remission of certain taxes like, Mangali, Sidhāyam kāṇike and
Vadivirāḷaṁ in Chukkalūru by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarājadēva Maharāja.

l.te: ṭi krō[dhi] saṁvatsara kā¨rttika śu 1[1] lu śrīmatu-

inscription.head:
No. 87.
(A.R. No. 9 of 1948.)
Jūṭūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Stone set up in the Chennakēśava temple.

This is dated Śaka 1449, expressed in chronogram nava-vārdhi-vēda-śaśi Sarvajit,
Chaitra ba. 6, Ina-sūnu-vāra corresponding to 1527 A.D., March 23, Saturday °70.

Records the gift of some lands as Sarvamānya in the village Pena-juṭūru situated in
the Gutti-sīṁa, for conducting different kinds of rituals associated with the processional deities
of the gods Kēśava and Paramēśvara by Daḷavāyi Timma-Māhēśvara, brother of Kṛishṇama
Nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 88.
(A.R. No. 10 of 1948.)
Jūṭūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Pillar set up in the Chennakēśava temple.

This is dated Śaka 1444, Chitrabhānu, Bhādrapada śu. 15, Friday, lunar eclipse
corresponding to 1522 A.D. September 5, Friday, lunar eclipse, °94.

Records the gift of certain taxes by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara, svamidroharagaṇḍa Pedda
Timmarāju, son of Varadayyadeva and Pāpasānamma. It also records the installation of the
Utsava images. The gift for the conduct of the festivals, offerings and perpetual lamp for
the merit of his parents on the occasion of lunar eclipse to the god Kēśava at Jūṭūru, situated
in Pennabaḍi which was the nāyaṅkara assigned to him by Mahāpradhāna Sāḷuva Timmarasayya
administering Gutti-sīma. Further, it states that the donor constructed the maṇḍapa in front
of the temple.

l.te: pratiṣṭha śēyiṁci dēvuni muṁdari cāvaḍi māni maṁḍapa-
muṁnu kaṭṭiṁci daśamī mahōtsavālaku ye 1 ki[rmā]-

sēsi tamatalli pāpasānaṁmakuṁnu tama taṁḍri varada-

inscription.head:
No. 89.
(A.R. No. 11 of 1948.)
Jūṭūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Stone lying near the Āñjanēya temple.

This is dated in Śaka 1470, Kīlaka, Chaitra śu. 5 corresponding to 1548 A.D. March
14, Wednesday °72.

Damaged. Seems to record a gift of land as Sarvamānya to the god Hanumanta (for
the merit of Ramākrishṇayya) by the learned Mahājanas and the Vipravinōdin community
residing at [Jūṭū]ru situated in the Jagatāpi-Gutti-sīma. Kēśavayya and Gōvindayya figure as
signatories.

l.te: naku yē vipravinōdulu tappinānu mātṛgamanaṁ sēsinavāru

inscription.head:
No. 90.
(A.R. No. 12 of 1948.)
Kottūr (hamlet of Brāhmaṇapalle), Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Stone lying near the Āñjanēya temple.

Damaged. States that Śrījatukumeru built the temple after being permitted by Kaninā
son of Nāgināyaka. Mentions Koṇḍaya and Karaṇam Rāmaya. In characters of the 17
century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 91.
(A.R. No. 13 of 1948.)
Kottūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Stone lying near the Āñjanēya temple.

This is dated Śaka 1435, Bhāva, Kārtika ba. 30, Monday. Irregular. The cyclic and
the Śaka years do not tally.

Records a gift of land for having constructed the Sukhanāsi, garbhagriha and stone
maṇḍapa of the Perumāḷ temple at Perukalapāḍu to (the mason) Malōju, son of Sarapaṁjaram
Basuvōju, by Vōbili-Nāyaka son of Kampla Kanikināyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 92.
(A.R. No. 14 of 1948.)
Kottūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Broken stone lying in a field near the Āñjanēya temple.

Records a gift of land (Pannāsa) at Peṇukapaṟu to Upekumāra Śarman of Bhāradvāja
gōtra by Pullamukki Boḷa Kaṇamayāru, when the Bāṇa king was ruling the Vaṅganūr-
Vishaya.

inscription.head:
No. 93.
(A.R. No. 15 of 1948.)
Kummetta, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Slab lying near the dhvajastambha of the Chennakēśava temple.

This is dated Śaka 1475, Pramādīcha, Śrāvaṇa ba. 15, Thursday corresponding to
1553 A.D. August 9 Wednesday °33.

Records the gift of land as Sarvamānya to the god Chennakēśava for providing food
offerings and also for the merit of 24 castes in the village Kummetta surnamed as
Virapratāpa Krishṇarāyapuram situated in the Pennabaḍi-māgāṇi in the Jagatāpati-Gutti-sīma
by Chintayya, son of Chennāreḍḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 94.
(A.R. No. 16 of 1948.)
Kummetta, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Sides of a mutilated slab lying in the compound of the Śiva temple.

Damaged. Seems to record a gift of some land to the god Rudradēva(?). In characters
of about the 8th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 95.
(A.R. No. 17 of 1948.)
Niṭṭūru (Guḍipāḍu), Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Stone lying near the Chennakēśava temple.

This is dated Śaka 1438, Dhāta, Nija, Śrāvaṇa śu. 11 corresponding to 1516 A.D.
August 8, Friday °50.

Records the gift of a piece of land to the piper (nāgasarāla) Chanōja for service in
the temple of Chennakēśava of Niṭṭūru by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Gōparāju, son of
Tammayadēvachōḍa Mahārāju.

inscription.head:
No. 96.
(A.R. No. 18 of 1948.)
Peddapappūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Broken slab set up in the Kṛishṇasvāmin temple.

This is dated Śaka 1475, Pramādīcha, Kārtika śu. 12 corresponding to 1553 A.D.
October 18, Wednesday 52.

Records the gift of land situated in Penna-Māgāṇi-sīma of the Jagatāpi-Gutti-rājya to
the god Krishṇama for the conducting the festivals and offerings at Peda Papūru surnamed
Rāmachandrapura by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Mūrtirāju Viṭhalayya of Ātrēya-gōtra for the merit
of his mother Tirumalamma.

inscription.head:
No. 97.
(A.R. No. 19 of 1948.)
Pedda Yekkalūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Broken slab lying near the Chennakēśava temple.

This is dated Śaka 1436, Yuva, Māgha ba. 14, Thursday. The cyclic and Śaka years
do not tally. If the cyclic year is taken as correct to be the details would correspond to 1516
A.D., January 31, Thursday f.d.t. °74.

Records the grant of land in the village Peda Yakalūru for construction of Raṅgamaṇḍapam
and conducting food offerings to the god Mūlasthāna Mallikhārjuna by Peda Tirumalayya,
son of Pradhāni Sōmarasayya for the merit of Sāḷuva Timmarasayya, the king's minister. The
village which was situated in (the division of) Pennabaḍi included in the Jagatāpi-Gutti-sīma
was a nāyaṁkara assigned by the minister to the donor.

dattāṁ vā yō hareti vasuṁdharāṁ sa-
na pōduru maṁgaḷamahā śrī śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 98.
(A.R. No. 20 of 1948.)
Pedda Yekkalūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the Chennakēśava temple.

This is dated Śaka 1468, Parābhava, Kārttika śu. 12 corresponding to 1546 A.D.
November 5, Friday °22.

Seems to record a gift of umbrella to the god Vēṅkaṭēśvara at Peda yakalūru in
Jagatāpi-Gutti-sīma by Śrī Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāju, son of Śriṅgāyadēva mahārāju.

l.te: bhava saṁvatsara kā¨rtika śu 12 lu śrīmanma-

inscription.head:
No. 99.
(A.R. No. 21 of 1948.)
Pedda Yekkalūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Another Slab kept in the Chennakēśava temple.

This is dated Śaka 1604, Dundubhi, Chaitra śu. 10 corresponding to 1682 A.D. April
10, Friday.

Damaged. Seems to record a gift of land to the god Śri Mallikhārjuna at Pedayakalūru.
Other details are lost.

l.te: bhi saṁvatsara caitra su 10 lu śrīma-

inscription.head:
No. 100.
(A.R. No. 22 of 1948.)
Puliproddaṭūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Stone set up near the Rāmasvāmin temple.

This is dated Śaka 1499, Bhāva, Śrāvaṇa ba. 12, Vāmana-jayanti. Corresponding to
1577 A.D., August 12, Saturday.

Records the king's gift of the village Podaṭūru renamed Śrīraṅgarāyapuram in the
presence of god Hariharēśvara on the bank of the river Tuṅgabhadra as ēkabhōga-agrahāra
Sarvamānya to Tirumala Peddāchāryulu, son of Peda Tirumalāchāryulu and grandson of
Tirumala Pedaya·āchāryulu of the Shaḍamarusha-gōtra, Āpastaṁbha-sūtra and Yajuś Śakhā on
the occasion of Vāmana-jayanti. The king Śrīraṅga is stated to be the son of Tirumala and
grandson of Śrīraṅga of the Ātrēya-gōtra, Āpastaṁba-sūtra and Yajus-śākhā.

l.te: śrāvaṇa dvādasi vāmana jayaṁtti puṁṇyakālamaṁdu śrīmatu
du hariharēśvara sānnidhyamaṁdu vāmana jaya[ṁ]ti puṁṇyakā-

inscription.head:
No. 101.
(A.R. No. 23 of 1948.)
Puliproddaṭūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Stone lying in the village.
This is dated Śaka 1468, Parābhava ba. 30.
Incomplete. Ends abruptly after mentioning the date.

inscription.head:
No. 102.
(A.R. No. 24 of 1948.)
Sazzaladinne, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Chennakēśvara temple.
This is dated Śaka 1489, Prābhava, Jyēshṭha śu.

Damaged. Seems to record some concessions granted for bringing under cultivation
lands which had become fallow in the village Sajjaladinna which was an Uṁbaḷi-grāma (rent
free grant of a village). Mentions Rāmarāju Tirumalarājayya.

l.te: • • nigāru sajjaladiṁnnagrāmaṁ polaṁ sāgubaḍki veyiṁccina dha¨rmaśāsanakra-
• • nāyaniṁgāru māku vuṁbbaḷiki modulapeṭṭina sajjala dinne-

inscription.head:
No. 103.
(A.R. No. 25 of 1948.)
Sazzaladinne, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Āñjanēya temple.

This is dated Śaka 1466, Krōdhi, Karttika śu. 12 corresponding to 1544 A.D. October 27,
Monday: 61.

Records the remission of taxes viz., Maṅgali, Sidāyam-kāṇike and Veṭṭi-virāḷamu in the
village Penajaladina of Jagatāpi-gutti-durgam to Koṇḍōju, (a servant of the king) by
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Alluḍu Rāmarāja. It is interesting to note that Alluḍu Rāmarāja was
devoted to the feet of Lord Kṛishṇa.

l.te: gunēṭi krōdhi saṁvakṣara kā¨rtika su dha 12 lu śrīman ma-

inscription.head:
No. 104.
(A.R. No. 26 of 1948.)
Sazzaladinne, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the temple of Bilēśvara outside the village.

This is dated Śaka 1438, Dhātu, Vaiśākha Śu. 3, Akshaya Tṛitīya corresponding to
1516 A.D. April 5 Saturday °42.

Records the gift of the village Lakshmisamudram for conducting the daily worship
etc., and periodical festivals to the god Bilēśvara at Pennabaḍi-Sajjaladinne, situated in the
Gutti-rājya by Appayya and Gōpayya, sons of Nādiṁḍla Timmarāja for the merit of Sāḷuva
Timmarasayya and his wife Lakshmamma. The donors who were the nephews of the famous
minister Sāḷuva Timmarasa founded the village in the name of the latter's wife and got
constructed the garbha-gṛiha, sukanāsi, antarāla and Raṅgamaṇḍapa of the temple on the
occasion of Akshaya Tṛitīya.

inscription.head:
No. 105.
(A.R. No. 27 of 1948.)
Tāḍpatri, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Slab built into the wall to the right of the main entrance of the Chintalarāya temple.

Records the homage of Pōtarāja-Liṁgarāja, son of Pōtarāja Yallamarāja of Tāḍiparti
to the god Chintalarāya. In characters of about the 17th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 106.
(A.R. No. 28 of 1948.)
Tāḍpatri, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Slab built into the left wall of the main entrance of the same temple.
This is dated Ānanda, Mārgaśira ba. 5.

Registers a monetary transaction between Gutti Tirumala Nāyaka and Koṇidela
Chainna-kṛishṇama-Nāyaka on one side and Vōbaya, son of Chinnamaseṭṭi on the other.

inscription.head:
No. 107.
(A.R. No. 29 of 1948.)
Tāḍpatri, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Slab built in the premises of the local High School.

This is dated Śaka 1481, Siddhārthi, Adhika Āśvayuja śu. 10, Monday corresponding
to 1559 A.D. September 11, Monday °29.

Seems to record a gift (not clear) to the god Śrīraṅgarāja of Ālūrikōna. Mentions
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Sidhirāju Timmarājayyadēva Mahārāja, on whose orders, Timmarasayya,
the pārupatyaṁ of Ālūru made proclamation.

l.te: gāru śēsina kaṭaḍi pu¨rvāna naḍapica cavalori ceruvu munagapalle grāmālapu-
2 nīvu vāsiga 22 • yaḍamarāju ·ālūri polaṁlōnu pāttaṁ ceruvutoṭa-

inscription.head:
No. 108.
(A.R. No. 30 of 1948.)
Yāḍiki, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Slab built into the pavement inside the Chennakēśava temple.

This is dated Śaka 1754, Nandana, Mārgaśira śu. 15, corresponding to 1832 A.D.
December 7, Friday °82.

Records a gift (not specified) by Robertson, Principal collector of Bellary to the god
Chennakēśava of Yāḍaki.

l.te: mā¨rgaśira śu 15 la varaku śrīmadakhilāṁḍa kō-

inscription.head:
No. 109.
(A.R. No. 31 of 1948.)
Yāḍaki, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p:
Two slabs built into the wall to the proper right of entrance of the Āḷvār shrine
in the Chennakēśava temple.

This is dated Śaka 1484, Dundubhi, Kārttika śu. 12 (Tirkōṭi-Dvādasi) corresponding
to 1562 A.D. November 7, Saturday °96.

Damaged. Records the gift of lands to the temple of Śri Kēśavamūrtti at Yāḍaki in
Gutti-sīma by Tirumala-Nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 110.
(A.R. No. 93 of 1948.)
Chandalūru, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District.
inscription.p: Nandi-pillar in front of the Śiva temple.

This is dated Śaka 1175, Vaiśākha Saptami, Sunday (divasakṛid-vāramu)
corresponding to 1253 A.D. April 7, Sunday.

Partly built in. Seems to record the installation of an image of Garuḍa by a certain
Māraṇḍu of chaturtha-vaṁśa. It also mentions Mahāpradhāni Sarvādhikāri Māyidēva-pregaḍa,
an official under a chief (name lost) of Addaṅki, who was a subordinate of the Kākatīya
Gaṇapati.

inscription.head:
No. 111.
(A.R. No. 99 of 1948.)
Sannamūru, Podili Taluk, Nellore District.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the Śiva temple.

This is dated Śaka 930, Vaiśākha Puṇṇami, Friday corresponding 1008 A.D. April
23, Friday °14. There was a lunar eclipse on this day, not mentioned in the record. The cyclic
year was Kīlaka.

Commences with the Praśasti of Bāṇa king. Sakalajagatrayābhivandita,
Mahābalikulōdbhava etc., and records the gift of 24 maruturs of land by the chief to the god,
Agrastēśvara-Bhaṭāra of Annagūru for worship and offerings. Mentions Pedda-rājaya and
Koṇḍuka-rājaya.

inscription.head:
No. 112.
(A.R. No. B 140 of 1948.)
Bojjanakoṇḍa alias Saṅkāram, Anakapalle Taluk, Visakhapatnam District.
inscription.p: Cave No. 1, on the left jamb of the entrance.

Damaged. Seems to be the name of a person. In characters of about the
century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 113.
(A.R. No. 141 of 1948.)
Bojjanakoṇḍa alias Saṅkāram, Anakapalle Taluk, Visakhapatnam District. Cave No.
1, pillar to the right of the stūpa.
inscription.p: Damaged. In characters of about the 7th-8th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 114.
(A.R. No. B 142 of 1948.)
Andhavaram, Chicacole (Srikakulam) Taluk, Visakhapatnam District.
inscription.p: Slab on the mound known as Iñjamma.
This is dated Śaka 10[5]. Chaitra, Śukla-paksham, Saturday.

Damaged. Records the gift of lamp to the god Īśvaradēva at Aṅdhovaram by Yeṟayyama
daṇḍanāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 115.
(A.R. No. B 143 of 1948.)
Andhavaram, Chicacole (Srikakulam) Taluk, Visakhapatnam District.
inscription.p: Slab in front of the house of Putta Dālayya Dora in the village.
This is dated Śaka 101[1].
Damaged. Mentions date portion only.

inscription.head:
No. 116.
(A.R. No. B 144 of 1948.)
Purushōttamapuram, Chicacole (Srikakulam) Taluk, Visakhapatnam District.
inscription.p: Slab in the temple of Kāmēśvara.

This is dated in regnal year 4[5], Śaka 1048, Kanyā-saṅkrānti. corresponding to
1126 A.D. August 28, Saturday. However the cyclic year is Parābhava.

Damaged. Records the gift of 5 māḍas (gold coins) for a perpetual lamp to the god
Kāmēśvaradēva.

inscription.head:
No. 117.
(A.R. No. B 146 of 1948.)
Purushōttamapuram, Chicacole (Srikakulam) Taluk, Visakhapatnam District.
inscription.p: Slab in the temple of Kāmēśvara.
This is dated Śaka 1055, Tula (month) • • • • .
Registers provision made by a lady for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Kāmēśvaradēva.

inscription.head:
No. 118.
(A.R. No. B 147 of 1948.)
Purushōttamapuram, Chicacole (Srikakulam) Taluk, Visakhapatnam District.
inscription.p: Outside pillar, southern entrance, Kāmēśvara temple.
Damaged. Mentions the temple of Kāmēśvara and the gift of a lamp.

inscription.head:
No. 119.
(A.R. No. B 148 of 1948.)
Vajragoḍa, Golugonda Taluk, Visakhapatnam District.
inscription.p: Broken pillar on the vacant site of the village munsiff.
Damaged. Seems to mention Gaṇḍaramalla. In characters of about the 10th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 120.
(A.R. No. B 206 of 1950.)
Akkārappākkam, Tiruvallur Taluk, Chingleput District, Tamil Nadu.
inscription.p: Door-jamb of entrance into the maṇḍapa, Siva temple.
This is dated Pramōdūta, Vaiśākha śu. 2.
Damaged. Seems to records the construction of temple. Mentions Vēnkaṭēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 121.
(A.R. No. B 222 of 1950.)
Nannūrupāḍu, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District.
inscription.p: Stone pillar lying in front of the Nannūramma temple to the north-east of the village.
This is dated Śaka 1019, Vishuvu-saṅkrānti corresponding to 1097 A.D.

Registers the gift of the village Nannūru, free of all taxes, to the goddess Hōṅkāradēvi
of the village by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāyana-chōḍa maharāja, son of Ballaya-chōḍa of the
Telugu-choḍas.

inscription.head:
No. 122.
(A.R. No. B 223 of 1950.)
Nannūrupāḍu, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District.
inscription.p: Same pillar, second face.

Seems to record the gift of land to the goddess Hōnkaradēvi by Jogadobbagaṇḍa
Ballaya-Chōḍa of the Telugu-Chōḍa family, probably of land near the village of Nannūru as
Sarvanamasya for the up keep of the temple, in conformity with the tradition of this family
of chiefs of the solar race in respect of the temples in Pākanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 123.
(A.R. No. B 224 of 1950.)
Nannūrupāḍu, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District.
inscription.p: Same pillar, third face.
This is dated Śaka 1019, Mēsha, 1 corresponding to 1097 A.D. April 9 Wednesday.

Seems to refers to some gift probably of land (not specified), free of all levies, to the
goddess Hōṅkāradēvi of Nannūru by the king, whose ancestry for five generations, starting
from Chāgi Vaṁkana, his son Balle-rāju, his son Kaṉṭri Vaṁkya-rāju, his son Nanni-chōḍa
and his son Sūraparāju is given. Also mentions the god Chōḍēśvara-liṅga.

inscription.head:
No. 124.
(A.R. No. B 225 of 1950.)
Nannūrupāḍu, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District.
inscription.p: Same pillar, third face.

Seems to state that Bētana, the lord of the Chōḍas, obtained Nannūru on the banks of
the Guṇḍi. Also praises Komma of Kāśyapa-gōtra and the lord of Nannūru.

inscription.head:
No. 125.
(A.R. No. B 228 of 1950.)
Bhānumukkala, Banganapalle Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Pillar of the maṇḍapa in the Bhairava temple.
Purport not clear. In late characters.

inscription.head:
No. 126.
(A.R. No. B 229 of 1950.)
Anantarājupalle (hamlet of Komaravōlu), Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the Āñjanēya temple.
This is dated Yuva, Āshāḍha śu. 11. • • • • • • • • • .

It registers a gift of land for the amṛitapaḍi service of the god Hanumanta at
Gaṇapulapalle by Pina-koṇdappa Nāyudu, son of Veligōṭi Tirupati Nāyudu. The gift land is
said to be situated in Komaravōlu, granted as amara to the chief by Raṅgapatirāju.

l.te: yī puṇyānaku yavvaru dappinānu gaṁgaka¨rttanu gō-
vu brahmaṇa vadhiṁccina pāpāna bōvuvāru gāḍidi sisinaṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 127.
(A.R. No. B 230 of 1950.)
Anantarājupalle, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the Bhairava temple near the Dāmala-cheruvu tank bund.

This is dated Śaka 1511, Sarvadhārin, Māgha śu. 11 corresponding to 1589 A.D.
January 17, Friday, °29. However, the cyclic year was Virodhin.

Records the renovation of the tank Dāmala-cheruvu at Komaravōlu, said to have
been formerly built by the rasasiddhas and gone out of order, by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Apratīkamalla Pina Basuvarāja Dēvachōḍa Mahārāja of Reḍicherla, son of Sūraparāja Dēvachōḍa
Mahārāja and grandson of Pāpa Timmarāja Dēvachōḍa Mahārāja and a devotee of the god
Vallabharāya of Reḍicherla, and registers the restoration as Sarvamānya, of the former grants
of land under this tank to the temple and Brāhmaṇas in addition to further grants of land
made under another tank constructed by him after his name. The grants are stated to have
been made on the occasion of a solar eclipse in the year Vikṛiti, Āshāḍha, ba. 30. Tuesday.
Corresponding to 1590 A.D. July 21. Tuesday. However ba. 30 is somewhat odd.

inscription.head:
No. 128.
(A.R. No. B 231 of 1950.)
Bādinēnipalle, Banganapalle Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Stone in a field near Boḍḍuvānipalle.
This is dated Śaka 1499, Īśvara.

Damaged. Seems to refer to a grant of land made for the digging of a channel for
irrigation by Kumāra Timmā-Nāyuḍu. Mentions Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Jillēḷa Raṅgapatirājayya.

l.te: kāluva travvinaṁta mērānu mīku sahiraṇyōdaka dāna dārāpū-
rvakaṁgā dāravosi ·istimigāna yi kāluva tavviṁcci bāvineni-
yevaru tapinā kāśilōgōvu brāṁhmani vadhi·iṁccina pāpana bōvuduru

inscription.head:
No. 129.
(A.R. No. B 232 of 1950.)
Bādinēnipalle, Banganapalle Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Stone set up near the entrance to the Rāmasvāmin temple.

Records a gift of land as pannasa measured by the royal measure to Ālabōḷa Sabi
Śarma at Koṇḍuru by Bikramahādityarāja of Solar race and Kāśyapa-gōtra. In characters of
the 7th-8th century A.D.

l.te: svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vāyōharēti vasundharā ṣaṣṭiṁvariṣa sa(ha)srāṇi narakēpa¨rnyutētusaḥ

inscription.head:
No. 130.
(A.R. No. B 233 of 1950.)
Bādinēnipalle, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Stone set up at the entrance to the Rāmasvāmin temple.

Registers a gift of 4 puṭṭis of land as pannasa at Koṁḍlapalle to the priest
Rēvanabōḷu by Biki Rāja of solar race and Kāśyapa-gōtra. Seems to state that the receipient
of the gift had to pay as levy (ari) a quantity of grain as well as a gadyāṇa of gold and
a specified quantity of ghee. In characters of 7th-8th century A.D.

l.te: dīni sthiti[thi] dappi ceṟicina vāru va-

inscription.head:
No. 131.
(A.R. No. B 234 of 1950.)
Bādinēnipalle, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Stone slab at the near entrance of the Rāmasvāmin temple.

Damaged. Seems to mention Kōki. • • • Probably the donor and • • • • • rēbōḷu, the donee.
Also records a gift of land as pannasa. The details of the gift are lost. In characters of the
9th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 132.
(A.R. No. B 235 of 1950.)
Bādinēnipalle, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Stone set up at the entrance of the Rāmasvāmin temple.

This is dated Śaka 1477, Rākshasa, Pushya śu. 2 corresponding to 1555 A.D.,
December 15, Sunday. 92.

It registers the grant by the Vipravinōdins, Pedasōmuḍu of Chāṁgalamaṟṟi and
Gōvindayya and Mādhavayya of Chennūru, of their professional incomes to the learned
mahājanas of Bādinēnipalle alias Tirumalarājapuram situated in Kaṁbhaṁ-sīma. The gift was
made on the occasion of (makara) saṅkrānti.

l.te: japuraṁ stitāśēṣa vidvanmahājanālakuṁnnu yi saṁkrāṁti puṇyaṁ
la maṁdu vipravinōdulakiccē vata 9 nalu mīku trivācakaṁgā
śi yistimigānu mī putra pautra pāraṁparyamu ·anubhavi-
ṁcēdi yī puṇyānaku ye vipravinōdulu tappinānu

inscription.head:
No. 133.
(A.R. No. B 236 of 1950.)
Bādinēnipalle, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Pillar of the maṇḍapa in the compound of the Rāmasvāmi temple.

This is dated Śaka 1766, Krōdhi, Phālguṇa śu. 15 corresponding to 1844 A.D., March
4, Monday.

Records the construction of the temple of Śrī Rāma with its Kalyāṇamaṇḍapa by
Tirumalakoṇḍu, son of Gaṟṟe Koṇḍaḍu.

edition.p: The rest of the inscription is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 134.
(A.R. No. B 237 of 1950.)
Bōgōlu, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up in a field to the west of the village.

This is dated Śaka 1534, Paridhāvin, Chaitra Śu. 10 corresponding to 1612 A.D., April
30, Thursday.

States that Bhōgēvōlu Reḍḍi, son of Yellā Reḍḍi and grandson of Cheṅgi Reḍḍi
of the Māntakāla-gōtra, constructed a tank, well, garden and the Śiva temple.

inscription.head:
No. 135.
(A.R. No. B 238 of 1950.)
Cumbum, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Stone at the south end of the tank bund.

This is dated Śaka 1509, [Sarvajit], Bhādrapada ba. 30. corresponding to 1587 A.D.
September 22, Friday.

Records the renovation of the tank bund at Kambham, probably by Ghaṇḍi
Kūnama Nāyaniṁgāru, and registers gifts of land made to several individuals who seem to
have taken part in the renovation work. Mentions Koṇḍaya, Tippaya and Bhogayya.

l.te: kha • • • tra • • • • • ku peḍakaṁbhāna kha 170 nu-
mānyaṁ cenu 4 • 2 yisti yī vrāsina mēra •

inscription.head:
No. 136.
(A.R. No. B 239 of 1950.)
Cumbum, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Stone under a tree near the Inspection Bungalow, on the tank bund.
This is dated Śaka 1727, Kāḷayukta, Nija-Śrāvaṇa ba. 12, Friday, Irregular.

It contains the verse Mad-vaṁśajāḥ etc., and states that the verse was caused to be
engraved by Pedda Viraṇṇa and Mahānandi Chinna Viraṇṇa, sons of Pāpanna. Mentions the
god Sāraṅgēśvara.

l.te: ṭi kāḷayukta nāma saṁvvatsara nija śrāva-

inscription.head:
No. 137.
(A.R. No. B 2 of 1950.)
Cumbum, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Stone under a tree near the Inspection Bungalow, on the tank bund.
This is dated 1852 A.D., May.

Records that the north wall of the north shrine of the Cumbum tank was repaired
under the supervision of Major De Butts, Civil Engineer, 2nd Division. Mentions Peddakaṁaṇam
Sundara Mudaliyār, the writer of Major De Butts, Araṇi Kandappa the Maistry, Ayyāsāmi
Mudaliyār, the Taluka Maistry and the engraver of the record Rāmayya (son of), Dattayya.
Also refers to Nikhāsāṁ Khān and Vīrama Rājaṁma.

l.te: sāva 1852 saṁ || me nelalō reṁḍoḍivijan śivilu-

inscription.head:
No. 138.
(A.R. No. B 243 of 1950.)
Eṟṟabālem, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Pillar of a maṇḍapa at the foot of the Rāmalingēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1530, Rākshasa, Kārttika śu. 11 corresponding 1608 A.D.,
November 8 Tuesday.

Records the consecration of the images of the deities Lakshmī and others and the
setting up of the pillars for the swing (uyyāla) by Kannelūri Pāpaya Aṅkamaṁgāru at
Brahmaguṇḍam in Yeṟṟabālem.

inscription.head:
No. 139.
(A.R. No. B 244 of 1950.)
Eṟṟabālem, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p:
Slab set up on the hillock called dhanam-tippa near the 14th mile-stone on the way
from Cumbum to Rēcherla.
This is dated Śaka 999, Piṅgala corresponding to 1077-78 A.D.

Registers a gift of 2 maruttus of wet land (under the tank) and 7 puṭṭis (of dry land)
at Velivōlu by the king to the temple on the tank bund constructed by Rājēndrachōḍa-seṭṭi.
The endowment was entrusted to the followers of the sthānapati Śāntaśiva Paṇḍita.

inscription.head:
No. 140.
(A.R. No. B 245 of 1950.)
Eṟṟabālem, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab bearing a portrait image in high relief, near the inscribed slab above.

States that this is (the image of) Rājēndrasōḍa-seṭṭi who caused the construction of
this temple and the tank.

inscription.head:
No. 141.
(A.R. No. B 246 of 1950.)
Gōpanipalle (hamlet of Komaravōlu), Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Pillar of the Verandah in the Gōpinātha temple.
This is dated, Sādhāraṇa, Mārgaśira śu. Monday.

Records the obeisance of Śrīnivāsa, son of Bāsi Rāju to the god Gōpinātha of
Komaravōlu. In late characters.

inscription.head:
No. 142.
(A.R. No. B 247 of 1950.)
Gōpanipalle (hamlet of Komaravōlu), Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Garuḍa pillar in front of the Gōpinātha temple.
This is dated, Sarvadhāri, Vaiśākha ba. 6.
States that Bāraṁ-gōpu installed the Garuḍa (pillar). In late characters.

inscription.head:
No. 143.
(A.R. No. B 248 of 1950.)
Gōpanipalle (hamlet of Komaravōlu), Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab in front of the Veṇugōpālasvāmin temple.

This is dated Śaka 147[2], Sādhāraṇa, Mārgaśira ba. 12 corresponding to 1550 A.D.
December 5, Friday.

Damaged. Seems to record the remission of tax on the barbers in the time of Pina
Aubhaḷayyadēva Mahārāja of Naṅdyāla.

inscription.head:
No. 144.
(A.R. No. B 250 of 1950.)
Mohideenpuram (hamlet of Bōgōlu), Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Image of Hanumān in front of the Chennakēśava temple.
This is dated Śaka 1464, Dundubhi, Māgha ba. 13, Thursday, Irregular.

Records the consecration of the image of Hanumān at Bhōgavōlu by Chennama
Nāyuḍu, son of Paḍāla Andanayya.

inscription.head:
No. 145.
(A.R. No. B 251 of 1950.)
Mohideenpuram (hamlet of Bōgōlu), Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p:
Slab in front of the Bhairava temple on the Bhairavakoṇḍa hill, about 5 miles N.W.
of the village.

States that Kalli-boḷa made the images of Gaṇapati, Nandīśvara and Chaṇḍīśvara and
that Munasaṅga, son of Eyuva-Āchāri, engraved the inscription. In characters of about the 8th
century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 146.
(A.R. No. B 252 of 1950.)
Mohideenpuram (hamlet of Bōgōlu), Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab in front of the Bhairava temple on the Bhairavakoṇḍa hill.

This is dated Śaka 1324, Chitrabhānu, Āshāḍha ba. 3 Sunday corresponding to 1402
A.D. June 18, Sunday.

States that Bommi Reḍḍi, son of Siṅgāya reḍḍi of Donakoṇḍa, renovated the temple
of Nīlakaṇṭhadēva on the Udayagiri hill in Ēṟuva-nāḍu for the merit of his father and mother
Chevasāni.

inscription.head:
No. 147.
(A.R. No. B 253 of 1950.)
Mohideenpuram (hamlet of Bōgōlu), Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Fragmentary slab kept in front of the Bhairava temple.
Damaged and fragmentary. In characters of about the 9th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 148.
(A.R. No. B 253 of 1950.)
Mohideenpuram (hamlet of Bōgōlu), Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Saptamāṭrikā panel kept in front of the Bhairava temple.
Damaged. Seems to mention Mahēndra Chōṟa. In characters of the 10th-11th century A.D.

l.te: [śrī] • • • tamāhēdracō(vī)ra ba • • • siva • • • padasaṇaja • • ndavija • • • vva • ḥ

inscription.head:
No. 149.
(A.R. No. B 254 of 1950.)
Mohideenpuram (hamlet of Bōgōlu), Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Threshold slab of the goddess's shrine of the Bhairava temple.

Damaged. Seems to record the obeisance of some devotees whose images are embossed
in flat relief in the act of prostrating before the god. Mentions Tatvaprakāśa-Yōgi. In late
characters.

inscription.head:
No. 150.
(A.R. No. B 255 of 1950.)
Mohideenpuram (hamlet of Bōgōlu), Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p:
Brim of the natural pond called Śāsanāladona, about 1/2 a mile to the north of the
Bhairava temple.

It contains the birudas Śri Utpātipiḍugu and Ēkantanivāsi. In characters of about the
8th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 151.
(A.R. No. B 256 of 1950.)
Mōkshaguṇḍam, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Door-jamb, proper right, of the Muktīśavara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1691, Sarvadhārin, Māgha śu. 15 corresponding to 1769 A.D.
February 20, Monday.

Records the completion of the garbhagṛiha and antarāḷika of the temple of Mukteśvara
on the date specified. Registers a gift of one Kuñcha of land in Mōkshaguṇḍam to god
Sāṁbaśiva by Tāḍicherla Veṅkaṭanarasu for conducting food offerings and perpetual lamp on
the occasion of Śivarātri to the god.

inscription.head:
No. 152.
(A.R. No. B 257 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the Surabhēśvara temple.

This inscription states that the mother of Ujēnīpisācha gave a grant of land measuring
150 (Nivartanas) to Gōvṛishāṇa-Bhaṭṭāraka. (pub. Ep.Ind. Vol. XXIX pp. 160 ff).

l.te: mātā dattaṁ gōvṛṣāṇa bhaṭṭārahōśata paṁcāśat kṣētram
svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā yō harēti vasuṁndharā

inscription.head:
No. 153.
(A.R. No. B 258 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the Pāpavināśēśvara temple.
This is dated Śaka 1569 • • • śu. 15, Monday corresponding to 1647 A.D.

Damaged. Records the gift of a gold plate, 2000 ṭaṁkas, horses and umbrellas to the
god Mallikhārjunadēva by Sthānapati Pedagaṅga-Jiyya. Further, it records the gift of some
lands in the village Malkāyapalli to the gods Surabhēśvara, Pāpavināśadēva and Rāmayadēva
of Sattevōlu and records the levy of fee on marriages and Vaḍugu (U panayanam)-ceremonies
performed in these three temples.

l.te: • ni reṁḍuvēla ṭṭaṁkālu celliṁstēnu peṭṭina tējamāṁ-
ṭiṁnni varimaḍi yinu maruturu nīrunēlānu vrittigā-

ni koḍuku kāse mayesvari vēsina sasanaṁ

inscription.head:
No. 154.
(A.R. No. B 259 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the Pāpavināśēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1462, Sārvari, Āshāḍha śu. 11, Tuesday corresponding to 1540
A.D., June 15, Tuesday.

Registers the gift of the village Pōtavaraṁ near Tamataṁkoṇḍa in Ēṟuva-sīma by
Nāgappaṅgāru, son of Paṇḍari Rāmabhaṭlaṁgāru of Kāśyapa-gōtra, for the daily worship and
conduct of Śodachopachara (16 kinds of homage) to the god Pāpavināśanadēva and for the
merit of Venkaṭādri-Ayya, son of Yaṅddaluri Timmā-jōśya of Vishṇuvardhana-gōtra, Āśvalāyana-
sūtra and Yajus-śākhā, and governor of Udayagiri, who is said to have conferred the Ēṟuva-
sīma as Pārupatyam on the donor.

inscription.head:
No. 155.
(A.R. No. B 259 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the Pāpavināśēśvara temple.
This is dated . • • • • śu. 15, Monday.

Damaged. Registers the gift of the village Vimjyanamupāḍu in Vinikoṇḍa-dēśam to the
god Pāpavināśanadēva at Anu (?) for conducting aṁgaraṁgabhōga in Ēṟuva-sīma by the king
for the health and prosperity of his father Harihararāya and himself. A post-script dated
Vibhava, Kārttika śu. 10, Sunday, records the sale of half of his share by Surabha-Jiya, son
of Guruga-Jiya to Nāra-Jiya (son of Pāpa-Jiya).

l.te: mahā śrī śrī śrī svadattā dviguṇaṁ pu-
ṇa svadattaṁ niṣphalaṁ bhavet | svadattāṁ parada-
ttāṁ vā yō harēta vasuṁddharā | ṣaṣṭi¨rvva-

inscription.head:
No. 156.
(A.R. No. B 261 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: On a pedestal of a votive liṅga in the Pāpavināśēśvara temple.
Mentions Nagasatti. In characters of about the 9th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 157.
(A.R. No. B 262 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: On a pedestal of a votive liṅga (2) in the Pāpavināśēśvara temple.
Damaged. Mentions Śridhaṟa. In characters of the 9th-10th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 158.
(A.R. No. B 263 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: On a pedestal of Votive liṅga (3) in the Pāpavināśēśvara temple.

Damaged. Mentions Śrī Ṟaijara and Vallabha. In characters of about the 9th century
A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 159.
(A.R. No. B 264 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Sides of an image of Pārvatī near the ruined temple of Pāpavināśēśvara.

States that Vīrayarāju, son of Timmarāju and the karaṇam of Turimella dedicated the
image of Pārvatī to the god. In characters of 17th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 160.
(A.R. No. B 265 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: A sculptured slab near the temple of Surabhēśvara.

Damaged. Mentions Siṅgadēva, the son of Bairavadēva and the former's servant (name
not clear) who is probably the person depicted in the sculpture.

inscription.head:
No. 161.
(A.R. No. B 266 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: A sculptured slab near the temple of Surabhēśvara.

Damaged. It seems to specify the person depicted in the sculpture. In characters of 12th
century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 162.
(A.R. No. B 267 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Broken slab near the Surabhēśvara temple.
This is dated Śaka 1164 [1242 A.D.], other details are damaged.

Fragmentary. Introduces Gaṇḍapeṇḍāra Gaṅgaya-Sāhiṇi who bears the epithet Arivīra-
Brahmarākshasa and is said to have been ruling over Ēṟuva-Three-seventy division from the
capital town of Krochcherula. The rest of the record is broken and lost.

edition.p: The rest is broken and lost.

inscription.head:
No. 163.
(A.R. No. B 268 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Broken slab, side face near the Surabhēśvara temple.
This is dated Krōdhi, Phālguṇa śu. 15, Monday.

Damaged. Purport not clear. Seems to refer to some gift of land for the merit of
Vennasāni. In characters of about the 13th century A.D.

edition.p: The rest of the inscription is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 164.
(A.R. No. B 269 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: On a sculptured stone, backside, near the Surabhēśvara temple.

Damaged. The first portion of the inscription mentions only the imprecatory verse,
while the second portion refers to some cowherd community. In characters of about the 10th
century A.D.

At the left hand side of the record, images of a man and a woman with a sword in
her hand, are portrayed below the figure of an animal. These two figures may be taken to
represent the members of the cowherd community.

inscription.head:
No. 165.
(A.R. No. B 270 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Surabhēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1462, Sārvari, Āshāḍha śu. 11, Tuesday corresponding to 1540
A.D., June 15, Tuesday.

Registers the grant of the village Chōḷavīḍu, renamed Surabhēśvarapuram by Nāgappaṁ-
gāru, son of Paṇḍari Rāmābhaṭlu to the god Surabhēśvara. The gift village situated in Ēṟuva-
sīma was held as Pārupatyam by the donor, Venkaṭādri-Ayya, son of Yiṁḍḍetūri Timma-
Jōśyulu and nephew of Bhūtanāṁtani Rāmābhaṭlayyavāru, who is described as the rājyabhāra-
dhuraṁdhara of the king, and is stated to be governing Udayagiri-Durgam.

inscription.head:
No. 166.
(A.R. No. B 271 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab build into the steps behind the Kalyāṇa-maṇḍapa at the Surabhēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1475, Virōdhikṛit, Kārttika śu, 11. corresponding to 1553 A.D.
October 17, Tuesday (the cyclic year was Pramādi).

Damaged. Seems to register a gift of certain villages in the Ēṟuva-sīma to the god
Surabhēśvara by Daḷavāyi Veṁgaḷanāyuḍu, the Karyakartta of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Eṟṟa-
Tirumalayyadēva Mahārāya.

inscription.head:
No. 167.
(A.R. No. B 272 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Base of the Saptamātṛika panel in Surabhēśvara temple.
Slab built into the steps behind the Kalyāṇa-maṇḍapa at the Surabhēśvara temple.
This is dated Śaka • • • • ., Hēmaḷambi, Jyēshṭha śu. 15, lunar eclipse. Irregular.

Damaged. Records the gift of villages in the Ēṟuva-sīma by Kaṭhāri Sāḷuvarāya (name
not mentioned) the son of Sāḷuva Timmayadēva-Mahārāja to the god Surabhēśvara for the
merit of the king, Rāmābhaṭlayya and his father Timmarāja and also for the health and
prosperity of donee.

inscription.head:
No. 168.
(A.R. No. B 273 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Base of the Saptamātri panel in Surabhēśvara temple.
Damaged. Mentions Ēṟuva-Mahādēvi. In characters of about the 7th century A.D.

l.te: śrī paramāṇḍa vīṟatu śrīpradhāna kumāruḍḍu prakā • • pu • hā • • • • • • • • • • nu ·eṟuva mahādēvi

inscription.head:
No. 169.
(A.R. No. B 274 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p:
Slab near the Pālakola by the side of the Gaṇēśa image on the hill, opposite the
Surabhēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1181, Kāḷayukta, Kārttika ba. 1, Tuesday, lunar eclipse corresponding
to 1258 A.D. November 1, Tuesday, Lunar eclipse occurred previous day.

Records a gift of land made by Mahāpradhāni and Sarvādhikāri Anantadēva to the
god Muktēśvara for the merit of the king. It also records another gift of 5 ga of land by
Paḍihāri Baḷiyappa-Nahiḍu (Nāyuḍu) for the burning of lamp and bells for the same deity.
Further it states that Mahādēvarāśi, the son of Iśānarāśi was placed incharge of the
endowments. It contains very interesting imprecatory portion that whoever destroys this
charity would commit the sin of falling into Kumbhipākam (ethereal hell or infernal hell).

inscription.head:
No. 170.
(A.R. No. B 275 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Fragmentary slab lying in the Vīrabhadra temple.
This is dated Pārthiva, Vaiśākha śu. 15, Monday.

Seems to records the installation of the deities Brahma, Vishṇu, Mahēśvara and
Gurakka (village deity) by some chief (name lost) for the merit of his father Bommarāju and
his mother (name lost). In characters of about the 17th century A.D.

edition.p: The rest of lines are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 171.
(A.R. No. B 276 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Slab set up infront of the Vīrabhadra temple.

This is dated Śaka 1467, Viśvāvasu, Kārttika śu. 11, Monday, corresponding to 1545
A.D., October 16, Friday.

Records the grant of land in Turimiḷa of Ēṟuva-sīma for food offerings and
aṁgaraṅgabhōgas to the god Bhīmēśvara by Siṅgarayyadēva Mahārāja, son of Avubhaḷayyadēva
Mahārāja of Pāṇem. Turimella is stated to have been given as amaranāyaṅkara to the donor
by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Nandyāla Aubhalayyadēva Mahārāja.

l.te: jāyatē krimi || śrī śrī śrīn jēyu

inscription.head:
No. 172.
(A.R. No. B 278 of 1950.)
Turimella, Cumbum Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p:
Slab in a field on the way to Mādhavāpuram-Śrōtriyam, about 1(1/2) miles N.E. of the
village.
This is dated Śaka 1314, Āṅgīrasa, Āshāḍha śu. 15, Friday. Irregular.

Damaged. Records the construction of a tank and the assignment of lands under it to
various families who took part in its construction including the Karaṇams, in Turimella when
Kaṭhāri Sāḷuva Guṇḍayadēva Mahārāja was ruling over Tuṟimiḷa-rājya and Dēvaraya, son of
the king, was ruling over the Udayagiri-rājya.
