l.note: This line engraved on the top of the inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 173.
(A.R. No. B 285 of 1950.)
Pālūru, Rapur Taluk, Nellore District.
inscription.p: Nandi-pillar lying between this village and Anantamaḍugu.
This is dated Śaka 1220, Mēśha 22, Vaiśākha śu 3, Saturday, Rōhiṇi. Irregular.

Records the gift of land to the god Śrīraṅganātha by the king. Further, it refers to the
construction of a maṭha in the garden of Soṁi-reḍḍi located near Nallaricheruvu in Pāluru
in Pālūru-nāḍu in Pāka-nāḍu by Rājagaṇḍa-gōpaḷa and gifts to the god Mallināthadevara by
Siddhayadēva-mahārāja, son of Ballirāja of Solar race.

inscription.head:
No. 174.
(A.R. No. B 286 of 1950.)
Yallūru, Sulurpet Taluk, Nellore District.
inscription.p:
Slab set up in a field about two furlongs north of the village in the limits of
Gaṇḍarasiṅgaṁpalle-Khaṇḍrika.
This is dated Vikṛiti, Āshāḍha śu. 11.

Registers the assignment of 5 Kuṇṭas of land under the tank at Gaṇḍrasiṁgaṁpalle and
a levy of 1 Kuñcha for every aḍḍa of grain, probably for the maintenance of the tank by
Dāmara Lingappaṁgāru.

inscription.head:
No. 175.
(A.R. No. B 287 of 1950.)
Kaḍaguṇṭa, Venkatagiri Taluk, Nellore District.
inscription.p: Stone fixed into the sluice of the tank called Nāyani-Cheruvu.

This is dated Śaka 1095, Rudhirōdgāri, Vaiśākha śu. Ēkādaśi. [Though the week-day
is not mentined it is A.D. date to be given April 25, Wednesday. The cyclic year was Vijaya.]

Damaged. Seems to records a gift of land by Kāḷama-nāyaka to some brāhmaṇas as
an agrahāra for the merit of his parents. The donor is stated to be the sarvant (baṇṭu) of
Paṇṭa Nalla-siddhirāju. Vijādēva-mahārāja is mentioned as the ruler.

inscription.head:
No. 176.
(A.R. No. B 188 of 1951.)
Bōdeyapalle, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Pillar in front of the Chennakēśava temple.

This is dated Śaka 1436, Bhāva, Mārgaśira ba. 12, Wednesday, corresponding to 1514
A.D. December 13, Wednesday °19.

Records that Malidēvarāju, son of Sarasvati Tirumalarāju acting on behalf of the
twenty-four families of the Dommari community, granted to the god Chennakēśvara, the
dommari pannu, due from the Peñchukaladinne-agrahāra, surnamed Bukkarāyapuram of
Penna-māgāṇi in Gutti-rājya, on the occasion of Uttarāyaṇa-Saṅkrānti.

inscription.head:
No. 177.
(A.R. No. B 189 of 1951.)
Gōparājupalle, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Engraved on a stone kept in front of the Vishṇu temple.
This is dated Śaka 1436, Bhāva, Bhādrapada ba. 30, corresponding to 1514 A.D.
Damaged. Mentions Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Lakkarājayyadēva Maharāja.

inscription.head:
No. 178.
(A.R. No. B 190 of 1951.)
Gōparājupalle, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Boundary stone in Vaṅka (stream), west of the village.

States that as (the stone indicates) it is the boundary of Senagalagūḍūri-agrahāram
granted by Achyutadēva-Mahārāya to Narasiṁha Sōmayājin.

inscription.head:
No. 179.
(A.R. No. B 191 of 1951.)
Puṭlūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Stone (proper right side of the entrance) in the Vighnēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1468, Parābhava, Mārgaśira śu. 10, Thursday corresponding to
1546 A.D. December 2, Thursday, °86.

Seems to register a gift made to the gods Chennakēśava and Mūlasthānam
Bhōgēśvara at Puṭlūru, a village included in Pennabaḍi-māgāṇi of the Gutti-rājya. Also
mentions Ayyāvāḷi (merchant guilds) community. Further, it records a gift of land to gods
Kēśavanādha and Bhōgēśvara at Puṭlūru by Lakshmayya, son of Gaṅgarāju for the merit of
Bōyas and Gaṅgirāju.

inscription.head:
No. 180.
(A.R. No. B 192 of 1951.)
Puṭlūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Stone, proper left side of the entrance, same temple.
This is dated Śaka 1466, Krōdhi, Kārttika • • • • • .
Damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 181.
(A.R. No. B 193 of 1951.)
Puṭlūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Stone, in the Chennakēśava temple.
This is dated Śaka 1476, Ānanda, Nija-Āshāḍha ba. • • • • .
Damaged. The details are lost.

edition.p: Second Side and Third sides are damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 182.
(A.R. No. B 194 of 1951.)
Senagalagūḍūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: On a dhvajastaṁbha in the Chennakēśava temple.

Mentions Mārakāpuram Chenna[rāya] (i.e., the god Chennakēśava) and some preceptor
whose praśasti commences with the words Vēdamārga-pratishṭhāchārya etc., In late characters.

l.te: lina kēśavula ro[me] gūḍūrulō vuṁḍē ga-
ki daka • • • • • • • • • • mēra ·alā • na • ye pu-

inscription.head:
No. 183.
(A.R. No. B 195 of 1951.)
Senagalagūḍuru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Stone near gādi bhāvi, on the road side, north of the village.
This is dated Śaka 142[9], Prabhava, Kārttika śu. 1[5], [Sunday], Irregular.

Records the gift of some lands in Mārkapuram by Mahāmanḍalēśvara Salakarāju
Timmayadēva Mahārāja, and refers to a tank in the village Gūḍūru.

inscription.head:
No. 184.
(A.R. No. B 196 of 1951.)
Senagalagūḍuru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Boundary stone near Āñjanēya temple.

States that (the stone indicates) it is the boundary of Rāmachandrapura-agrahāra in
south-western direction.

inscription.head:
No. 185.
(A.R. No. B 198 of 1951.)
Takkeḷḷapāḍu, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Boundary stone in the village.

States that (the stone marks) the western boundary of Senagalagūḍūru-agrahāra
belonging to Narasiṁha Sōmayāji.

inscription.head:
No. 186.
(A.R. No. B 199 of 1951.)
Timmāpuram, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Boundary stone south east of the village.

Damaged. Seems to state (that the stone marks) the boundary of the village (name
lost), belonging to the god Tāḍipatti Rāmēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 187.
(A.R. No. B 200 of 1951.)
Urachintala, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Stone in the village.

This is dated Śaka 162[7], Tāraṇa, Āshāḍha ba. 5, Monday corresponding to 1704
A.D. July 10, Monday, °54. The Śaka year was current.

Damaged. Seems to state that Sādutullā Asāri Handammīyā Sāhebu granted Ūrachintala
(surnamed as Veṇkkaṭam) in Avuku-sīma for the settlement of certain families.

l.te: llukaṭṭuku cēnu 2 stalaṁ • tila ganuka vā-

inscription.head:
No. 188.
(A.R. No. B 201 of 1951.)
Vaṅganūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Stone (No. 1) on the tank bund on the border of the village.

This is dated Śaka 1429, Prabhava, Kārttika śu. 12, Monday corresponding to 1507
A.D. October 17, Sunday (not Monday) °71.

Records that Siddapanāyani-Koppinēniṁgāru of the Anukūla-gōtra granted lands and
levies in Beḍudūru as Sarvamānya to Koṇḍaya Krishṇayya-Timmayya for defraying the
expenses during the festival occasions at Śrīparvata for the benefit of pilgrims. Also states that
Beḍadūru was given as nāyaṅkara to Koppīneni by Sāḷuva Timmarāju.

l.te: ri pōlamēra vāgu tū¨rpū cēnu kha 2

inscription.head:
No. 189.
(A.R. No. B 202 of 1951.)
Vaṅganūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Stone (No. 2) on the tank bund on the border of the village.
This is dated Śaka • • • • • Śōbhakṛit, Pushya ba • 1.

Mentions Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kaṭhāri-Sāḷuva Teluṅgarāya Mahārāya as ruling Beḍudūru
in Pennabaḍi and records some transaction, the details of which are not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 190.
(A.R. No. B 203 of 1951.)
Velamakūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Stone kept near the cowshed.
This is dated Śaka 737, Madyamaviśa Vijaya, Uttarāyaṇa-Saṅkramaṇa.

Registers a gift of 150 maruṭṭuru of land at Velmalūru by Vallava Salkalu to the god
Bhōgīśvara. The gift was entrusted to Komarasamula Śivakarabhavyalu.

inscription.head:
No. 191.
(A.R. No. B 204 of 1951.)
Velamakūru, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Stone at the entrance of the Āñjanēya temple.

This is dated Śaka 1451, Virōdhi, Āshāḍha śu. 11 corresponding to 1529 A.D., June
17, Thursday.

Records the installation of the deity Hamumantadēva at Velamakūru and also grant of
some lands in the village for providing food offerings, and for burning perpetual lamp to the
god by Rāghavaya of Konāvakoṇḍa. The gift village Velamakūru situated in Pennabadi of
Guttirājya was being held as nāyankara by Dāsapanāyani Ayyavāru (who functioned) as a
subordinate of Ādepa-Nāyaka, when Vākiṭi Ādepa-Nāyanimgāru was administering Gutti-rājya.
Refers to Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Lakkayadēvarāja and his uncle Salakarāju.

inscription.head:
No. 192.
(A.R. No. B 205 of 1951.)
Vīrapuram, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Stone kept in a flower-garden near Vēṇugopālasvāmin temple.
This is dated Śaka 1466 • • • • .

Damaged. Seems to record that remission of some tax in Pennabadi by Mahāmaṇdalēśvara
Allu[ḍu] Rāma [rāja].

inscription.head:
No. 193.
(A.R. No. B 206 of 1951.)
Vīrāpuram, Tadpatri Taluk, Anantapur District.
inscription.p: Stone near Chāvaḍi.

Mentions one Vīramarāju who probably founded Vīramarājupuram. In characters of
about 15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 194.
(A.R. No. B 238 of 1951.)
Laddagiri, Kurnool Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p:
Lying in a field called Nambayachēnu, on the road leading to Veludurti.
Damaged. States the extent of the land assigned to Dharmadēva. In characters of about 15th
century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 195.
(A.R. No. B 287 of 1951.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk, Triruchinapalli District, Tamil Nadu.
inscription.p:
Raṅganāthasvāmi temple, second prākara, south side wall of the jewel-room, facing the
south wall of the Chandanamaṇḍapa.

The labels read: Sāḷuva Rāmarāju, Sāḷuva Kōnapparāju, Sāḷuva Kommarāju, Vēmūri
Varadarāju and Murtirāju. In late characters.

inscription.head:
No. 196.
(A.R. No. B 294 of 1951.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk, Triruchinapalli District, Tamil Nadu.
inscription.p: Raṅganāthasvāmi temple, second prākāra, tiers of the Verandah, west side.

Badly damaged. Contains the praśasti of Krishṇadēvarāya describing his victories. Seem
to record an endowment of lands in the villages Muttarasanellūr, Peruṅgudi to some maṭha,
and also gift of some jewels set with precious stones to the deities. (Published in SII., vol.
XXIV, No. 392).

l.note: Built in writing being mostly below ground

inscription.head:
No. 197.
(A.R. No. B 298 of 1951.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk, Triruchinapalli District, Tamil Nadu.
inscription.p: Second prākāra, west wall of the Verandah behind the Nāyaka images.

This is dated Śaka 1492, Pramōdūta, Vṛiśchika śu. [pañcha]mi, Friday, Pūrvāshāḍha.
Irregular.

This is Bilingual inscription. The record is prefaced with a Sanskrit verse invoking the
blessings of god Raṅganāthasvāmi and a short Telugu prose passage stating that the inscription
records the gifts made by Achyutappa-nāyaka, born to Chevvappa-Nāyaka and Mūrti-amma
by the grace of the god Raṅganātha. The main inscription which is in Tamil, registers the
grant of 25 villages situated in Chōḷa-maṇḍalam as Poliḷyūṭṭu yielding an aggregate amount of
6200 poṉ to be utilized for various services on specified occasions for the deities. Among the
persons entitled for a share in the offerings are mentioned the donor's preceptor Śravinūtula
Śrīnivāsāchāryar, son of Tirumalai Tāttaiyaṅgār, Parāśarabhaṭṭar Ayyaṅgār, Tiruveṅgadāchāryār,
Viravalli Aṇṇayyangār and others. Below is given the Telugu text of the impression.

inscription.head:
No. 198.
(A.R. No. B 312 of 1951.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk, Triruchinapalli District, Tamil Nadu.
inscription.p:
Second prakara, east side, tiers of the Arjunamaṇḍapa, facing the east wall of the
Sēnaimudaliyār shrine.

Records the obeisance of Duggarāju Ayya of Pulindula (Pulivendula) to god Raṅganātha.
In late characters. Pub: S.I.I., Vol. XXIV, No. 615.

inscription.head:
No. 199.
(A.R. No. B 319 of 1951.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk, Triruchinapalli District, Tamil Nadu.
inscription.p:
Second prakara, east wall of the Sēnaimudaliyār shrine, pillar at the corner of the
east and north walls. No date.

Records the obeisance of Veṅgalaya and Sūraya, sons of Daḷavāyi Mallarasu of
Kāśyapa-gōtra of Gummaḍipūṇḍi. In late characters. Pub: S.I.I., Vol. XXIV, No. 617.

inscription.head:
No. 200.
(A.R. No. B 329 of 1951.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamil Nadu.
inscription.p:
Second prākāra, east wall of the Sēnaimudaliyār shrine, pillar at the proper right of
entrance leading to the inner shrine.

This is dated Śaka 1492, Pramōdōdūṁta, Vṛiśchika śu [pañcha]mi, Friday, Pūrvāshāḍha.
Irregular.

This is a bilingual inscription. The first portion of the record is in Telugu which
records the obeisance to the deity Raṅgadhāma. The second portion of the record is in Tamil.
Records the obeisance of Tavatīrtta Kōmārachakkaram of Uttamankōyil and Appi, the
brother of Rāmānujaṉ and the son of Kōnēri Appaṉ. In late characters. Pub. S.I.I., Vol.
XXIV, No. 620.

inscription.head:
No. 201.
(A.R. No. B 294 of 1951.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Second prākāra, East wall, three pillars (No. 2, 3, & 4) in the Chandana-maṇḍapa.

Records the obeisance of Virūparāja, son-in-law of Achyutappa-nāyini Murāri. • • .
Sūrama and Kōnēṭamma. This same inscription is repeated on all the pillars. In late
characters. Pub. S.I.I., Vol. XXIV, No. 623.

inscription.head:
No. 202.
(A.R. No. B 334 of 1951.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Second prākāra, East wall, near the Vishṇupāda.

Beginning lost. Records the gift of the sacred food offering of vaḍar to the goddess
Nāṁchāramma. Also enumerates certain ornaments such as gold chains, ornaments called
Indra-gōpas, caps worked with gold, bronze plates and torches to the god Raṅgarāja by
Akājamma. In characters of about 16th century A.D. Published, S.I.I., Vol. XXIV, No. 443.

inscription.head:
No. 203.
(A.R. No. B 341 of 1951.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Second prākāra, West wall, tiers of the base, north end.

This is dated Śaka [1438], Dhātu, Māgha ba. 11, Monday corresponding to 1517
A.D., February 16, Monday, 80. The nakshatra was Pūrvāshāḍha.

States that the king, having conquered Udayagiri, captured Ravutarāya-mahāpatra, and
having taken the hill and plain fortresses of Addaṅki, Vinikoṇḍa, Bellaṁkoṇḍa, Taṅgeḍa,
Kētavaram etc., captured alive Vīrabhadrarāya, son of Pratāparudra Gajapatirāya, Naraharidēva,
son of Kumāra Haṁvīra and performed tulāpurusha at Amarēśvara in Dharanikōṭa, returned
to Vijayanagara and started again on a campaign of conquest towards Kaliṅga, reached
Bējavāḍa, conquered Koṇḍapalli captured Praharāju Śiraśchandra-mahāpātra, Bōḍajana-Mahāpātra,
Bijilikhāṇu and others, took at a stretch all the fortresses of Telaṅgāṇya such as Anantagiri,
Uṇḍrakoṇḍa, Urlagoṇḍa, Arushupalli, Jallipalli, Kandikoṇḍa, Kappaluvāyi, Nallagoṇḍla,
Kaṁbhaṁmeṭṭu, Kanakagiri, Śaṁkkaragiri etc., and installed the jayastaṁbha at Siṁhādri-
poṭnūru. Also records the remission of taxes on some villages evidently granted to the temple
on the occasion. Published; SII., Vol. XXIV, No. 371.

inscription.head:
No. 204.
(A.R. No. B 347 of 1951.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: On the gold pot in the Śri Raṅganāthaswāmi temple.
This bears the date Vikṛiti, Bhādrapada ba. 2.

States that this (the gold pot), which weighs 12,515 varāha and was stolen by thieves,
was recovered, re-done and restored to the temple by Raṅgappa-oḍeya. It was stolen in the
year Vikṛiti, on the 2nd day of the dark fortnight of Bhādrapada, during the reign of
Vijayaraṅga Chokkanātha-nāyaka son of Viśvanātha-nāyaka. Tupākul Rāmakṛishṇappa-nāyudu
recovered and restored it to the temple again. Published: S.I.I., Vol., XXIV, No. 573.

l.te: • • pannereṁḍu vēlunnēnūṭapadihēnu varālayettu yī biṁde yevarainā ·āśiṁci yettitē

cōrakulu yettukonirāgā pāḷina kanipeṭṭi raṁggappa kāḷākatōḷavoḍayāri vāru maḷḷinni
lessacēyiṁcci sannidhiki ·aṁppina biṁde
l.note: i.e. pāḷyamuna; pāḷyaṁ stands for ūdaiyarpalayam.

l.te:
vikṛtināma saṁvatsara bhādrapada ba 2 lu śrīmadviśvanāthanāyani vijayaraṁgga cokkanāthanāyanivāri
dināla śrīraṁgganāyakula baṁgāru biṁde

inscription.head:
No. 205.
(A.R. No. B 348 of 1951.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: On the gold plated staff of golden Umbrella studded with precious stones.
This is dated Ananda, Chaitra śu. 10, Friday Prabhava Mārgaśira.

States that the golden umbrella was made for the god Śrīraṅganātha by Jiḍḍu
Viśvanātha-Nāyani and that it was repaired in Mārgaśīra in the year Prabhava, corresponding
to December 1927. In modern characters. Published; S.I.I., Vol., XXIV, No. 564.

inscription.head:
No. 206.
(A.R. No. B 349 of 1951.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: On the gold plated staff.

This is dated (Śaka 1656,) Ānanda, Māgha śu. 2, Tuesday corresponding to 1735
A.D. January 14, Tuesday f.d.t. °17.

inscription.head:
No. 207.
(A.R. No. B 350 of 1951.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: On another gold plated staff.

This is dated Śaka 1656, Ānanda, Māgha Śu. 2 Tuesday corresponding 1735
A.D. January 14, Tuesday f.d.t. °17.

States that the staff weighing 1658 va.ga (Varāha gadyāṇa) and 12 na was the gift
of Kṛishṇājamma. Published; S.I.I., Vol. XXIV, No. 567.

l.te:
śālivāhana śakābdaṁ 1656 ·ānaṁda nāma saṁvatsaraṁ mākha śdu vi ·aṁgārakavāraṁ
śrīraṁgganāyakuluku viśvanāthanāyani śrīcokkanāthanāyanayyavāri paṭṭamahiṣulayina
śrīmīnākṣaṁmmagāru rājyabhāraṁ śēyucuṁnnuṁḍḍagā kṛṣṇājaṁmmavubhayaṁ [baṁgāru] beta ki

inscription.head:
No. 208.
(A.R. No. B 351 of 1951.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchirapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: On gold milk bowl.

States that the gold bowl weighing 335(1/2) ga., was the gift of the chief for serving
milk to the god. Published; S.I.I., Vol. XXIV, No. 562.

l.te:
vikāri saṁvatsaraṁ māśinela śrīraṁgganāyakulaku pālu ·āragiṁpaśāyanu rājaśrī vijayaraṁgga-
cokkanāthanāyanivāri vubhayaṁ ·aparaṁji ginne vokkaṭṭiki varā yettu muṁnnūṭamuppai-
·ayudunnara varāyettu ga ga 335

inscription.head:
No. 209.
(A.R. No. B 352 of 1951.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Pair of solid gold-plate feet of the god.

States that this is the gift of the Vijayaraṅga Chokkanātha to the god. Published; S.I.I.,
Vol. XXIV, No. 574.

inscription.head:
No. 210.
(A.R. No. B 353 of 1951.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: On a gold spittoon (Paḍikkam).

States that this is the gift to god Kastūri Raṅganāyakulu by Vijayaraṅga Chokkanātha.
Published; S.I.I., Vol. XXIV, No. 575.

inscription.head:
No. 211.
(A.R. No. B 380 of 1951.)
Alagum, Puri District, Orissa.
inscription.p: Stone found in the local temple of Śiva called Gartēśvara.
This is dated 23rd year, ba. • • • Traya-ēkadasi, Sunday.

Mentions Vishṇuvardhana Vijayādityadēva Chakravarti, Lokamahādēvi and Rājarāja
and records some gift to the god Guṟtta-dēva at Arugumaguṭṭa.

inscription.head:
No. 212.
(A.R. No. B 128 of 1952.)
Gunḍlapalle, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District.
inscription.p: Stone in front of the dhvajastambha in the Śiva temple.

Registers a gift of land sowable with 4 puṭṭis of āḍlu by Kaḍeyarāju, the
servant of Vijayāditya. The gift is stated to have been made in the name of
Aytakava, the queen of Kali-viṭrāju. In characters of about the 8th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 213.
(A.R. No. B 129 of 1952.)
Gunḍlapalle, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District.
inscription.p: Stone near the Pōlēramma temple.

This is dated Śaka 1441, Pramādi, Kārttika śu. 15, Sunday, lunar eclipse corresponding
to 1517 A.D., November 6, Sunday, lunar eclipse.

Damaged. Seems to record a gift of land as sarvamānya at Gundlapalli by Koṇēnri
Yaṟaya.

inscription.head:
No. 214.
(A.R. No. B 130 of 1952.)
Saṅgamjāgarlamūḍi, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District.
inscription.p: Pillar in the courtyard, Saṅgamēśvara temple.
This is dated Śaka 1. • • • ., Pushya ba. • • • makara-sankrānti, Wednesday.

Registers an endowment for burning lamps in the temple of Chōḍēśvara-mahādēva at
Chēbrōlu by Ko[ṭa]-bōyaṇḍu, Komana-bōyandu and Kōṭana-bōyaṇḍu, sons of Boṁ[ḍikōṭa]
Eṛaya-seṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 215.
(A.R. No. B 131 of 1952.)
Saṅgamjāgarlamūḍi, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District.
inscription.p: Another pillar in the courtyard, Saṅgamēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 116[4], Pushya ba. 3, Wednesday corresponding to 1242 A.D.,
January 10, [The day was Saturday].

Registers an endowment for burning lamps in the temple of Chōḍēśvara-Mahādēva at
Chēbrōlu by Āyātabōyuṁḍu, son of Boṁḍḍa-kōṭa Eṟama-śeṭṭi.

l.te: dattaṁ vā yo harēti vasuṁdharā saṣṭiva¨rṣasahasrāṇi

inscription.head:
No. 216.
(A.R. No. B 132 of 1952.)
Tenāli, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District.
inscription.p: Pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Rāmaliṅgasvāmin temple.

Records the setting up of the pillar in the mukha-maṇḍapa of the temple of
Sōmēśvara at Nandivelugu. In characters of about the 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 217.
(A.R. No. B 133 of 1952.)
Tenāli, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District.
inscription.p: Another pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Rāmaliṅgasvāmin temple.

Records the setting up of the pillar in the Rāmēśvara-maṇḍapa by a person (name not
clear). In characters of about the 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 218.
(A.R. No. B 134 of 1952.)
Vijayavāda, Bezwada Taluk, Krishna District.
inscription.p:
Slab fixed in the south-east corner of the second landing on the Mallikārjuna temple
to the Kanakadurgā temple.

States that one Chaṭṭapa consecrated the image of Pārthīśvara on the Indrakīla hill by
the grace of Vishṇuvardhana, son of Vikramāditya. Published: S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 16.

inscription.head:
No. 219.
(A.R. No. B 154 of 1952.)
Nāṅgūr, Shiyali Taluk, Tanjore District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Jamb of the doorway (gōpura) of the Nārāyaṇa Perumāḷ temple.
Mentions Kaḍavēḍi Yaṟappa. In late characters.

inscription.head:
No. 220.
(A.R. No. B 160 of 1952.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Third prākāra of the Rāmaliṅgasvāmin temple, tiers proper left of entrance.

This is dated Śaka 149[4], Āngirasa, Jyēshṭha śu. 15, Monday corresponding to 1572
A.D., May 26, Monday f.d.t. °48.

Damaged. Mentions Anantayya, son of Nēbati Rāmayya and others and their visit to
the temple. Published: S.I.I., Vol. XXIV, No. 493.

l.te: yya timmayya nārā[ya]ṇayya sahāgānu kuṭuṁba sahitaṁgānū
vāri komāḷḷu rāmayya bośvajēśiri raghunāthayya narasa[yya]

inscription.head:
No. 221.
(A.R. No. B 170 of 1952.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Third prākāra of the Rāmaliṅgasvāmin temple, proper right of entrance, lowest tiers.

Records the obeisance of Koṇḍamma, daughter of Bōṁmmarāju. In characters of the
15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 222.
(A.R. No. B 171 of 1952.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p:
Third prākāra of the Rāmaliṅgasvāmin temple, proper right in the passage, south and
west walls.

This is dated Śaka 1572, Vikṛiti, Mārgaśira ba. 5, Sunday, uttar-āshāḍha Vriśchika,
Uttiraṭṭādi, Irregular.

This is probably bilingual and the summary is based on Tamil lines. Registers a
poliyūṭṭu gift by Cheruku Chennamanāyaka son of Veṅgaḷappa and grandson of • • ., for
various offerings on the occasion when the deity was brought and seated in the maṇḍapa in
the grove which was also granted by the donor to the deity. Published: S.I.I., XXIV, No. 533.

inscription.head:
No. 223.
(A.R. No. B 219 of 1952.)
Kāpasōmapuram, Sringavarapukota Taluk, Visakhapatnam District.
inscription.p: Stone called Dhanaṁrāyi in field S. No. 72-9A.
Mentions Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Pratāpa • • • • In characters of about the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 224.
(A.R. No. B 209 of 1953.)
Sholinghur, Walajah Taluk North Arcot District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Stone tub in the kitchen of Narasiṁhasvāmin temple on the hill.

Reads Kaṁdāḷappa. In characters of the 16th century A.D. Below the label are engraved
Teṉgalai mark, Śaṅkha and chakra.

inscription.head:
No. 225.
(A.R. No. B 211 of 1953.)
Sholinghur, Walajah Taluk North Arcot District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Āñjanēyasvāmin temple, west wall, inside the gōpura.
This is dated Śaka 1541, Siddhārthi, Chaitra śu. 10, [1619 A.D. March 15, Monday].

Records the construction of gōpura, prākāra, the steps leading to the pond, shrines for
the Saptaṛishis and Varadarāja-perūmaḷ at Ghaṭikāchala by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Chirigirirāju
Chiṉṉa-Timmarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 226.
(A.R. No. B 214 of 1953.)
Mārlamaḍiki, Alur Taluk, Bellary District, Karnataka.
inscription.p: Stone set up at the entrance of the village.

Damaged. Mentions Prauḍha-Dēvarāya and Krishṇadēvarāya. Gururāja (the preceptor)
Chandrabhūshaṇa is also mentioned. In characters of 16th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 227.
(A.R. No. B 268 of 1953.)
Ēlūru, Eluru Taluk, West Godavari District.
inscription.p: Lift side pillar (top) in the Vishṇumaṇḍapa, Fort at Gandhakam.

This is dated Śaka 1234, Parīdhāvi, Phālguna ba. 1, Monday corresponding to 1313
A.D. Febraury 12.

States that the leṅkas, Sahavāsis and others, who had come to Dendulūru on sthala-
tārkkaṇa (?), purchased land from Maṁchchana, son of Kēśava-śeṭṭi and granted to god
Bhīmanātha for daily offerings. The gift is stated to have been made for the merit of
Pratāparudradēva-mahārāja (Kakatīya king).

l.te: ṁccanaṁgāri cātanu pōtunnūri polēṭi ceṟvaṁ dakṣiṇaṁ vaḷita •

inscription.head:
No. 228.
(A.R. No. B 269 of 1953.)
Ēlūru, Eluru Taluk, West Godavari District.
inscription.p: Left side pillar in the Vishṇumaṇḍapa, Fort at Gandhakam.
This is dated Śaka 1127, Pushya Saṅkrānti • • • • .

Registers the gift of manya-siṁhapu-gadyāṇa (?) by Prōli-seṭṭi to the god Bhīmēśvara-
Mahādēva located on the east of Dendulūru.

inscription.head:
No. 229.
(A.R. No. B 270 of 1953.)
Ēlūru, Eluru Taluk, West Godavari District.
inscription.p: This is dated Śaka 1113. (Sudha-Kara-Indu-Hariṇāṅka).

Records is in verse, states that Paṇṭanāyaka Pōtaya built the stone maṇḍapa for Śiva
at Dendulūru.

inscription.head:
No. 230.
(A.R. No. B 271 of 1953.)
Ēlūru, Eluru Taluk, West Godavari District.
inscription.p: Right pillar (top) in the Vishṇumaṇḍapa, Fort at Gandhakam.
This is dated Śaka 1109. (Navati-rudra).

Records the gift of land for the maintenance of a lamp for auxiliary deities like
Vināyaka in the temple (not stated) by Mulukaṭi Pōtama-seṭṭi of Dhanēśa-vaṁśa.

inscription.head:
No. 231.
(A.R. No. B 273 of 1953.)
Ēlūru, Eluru Taluk, West Godavari District.
inscription.p: Right pillar (bottom) in the Vishṇumaṇḍapa, Fort at Gandhakam.

This is dated Śaka 1156, Āshāḍha śu. 15, Monday corresponding to 1234 A.D. June
12, f.d.t, 07.

Damaged. Seems to record a land donation made by some pious devotees of
Dendulūru to the god Bhīmēśvara Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 232.
(A.R. No. B 274 of 1953.)
Ēlūru, Eluru Taluk, West Godavari District.
inscription.p: Right pillar (bottom) in the Vishṇumaṇḍapa, Fort at Gandhakam.
This is dated Śaka 115 • • • Uttarāyaṇa Sankrānti • • • .

Records that provision was made by Dāsava-[setti], son of Sūri-seṭṭi for burning a
perpetual lamp in the temple of the god Bhīmēśvara of Dendulūru.

inscription.head:
No. 233.
(A.R. No. B 275 of 1953.)
Ēlūru, Eluru Taluk, West Godavari District.
inscription.p: Broken pillar lying at the Bazaar post office.

Damaged. Mentions Śrī Madavuṇḍu and Ṟēpidēva. In characters of about the 10th
century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 234.
(A.R. No. B 276 of 1953.)
Kanuparti, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District.
inscription.p: Stone slab near the threshold of Ēlēśvara temple.

Damaged. Contains Imprecatory verses apart from the name of the king. In characters
of about 8th century A.D. Published; NDI. II, Ongole No. 57.

inscription.head:
No. 235.
(A.R. No. B 277 of 1953.)
Kanuparti, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District.
inscription.p: Stone kept in front of the Sitārāmasvāmi temple.

This is dated Śaka 12[23], Plavaṅga Kārttika [śu 11], Friday, Śatabhisha corresponding
to 1301 A.D., October 13.

Records the gift of some lands under cultivation of Kāmasamudram (a tank of
Kanuparṭi) for defraying the expenses of worship and offerings to the god Malamaṇḍala-
perumāḷ of Mōṭṭupalli by Kāmaya-bōya. Published; NDI, Part II, No. 54.

inscription.head:
No. 236.
(A.R. No. B 278 of 1953.)
Kanuparti, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District.
inscription.p: Stone in the Sitārāmasvāmi temple.

This is dated Śaka 1348 (gaja-abdhi-jvalana-śaśi), Parābhava, Māgha śu. 11,
Thursday corresponding to 1427 A.D. January 9.

States that the chief Aubhaḷa-kshāpati, son of Nūṁkabhūpāla remitted certain taxes on
the temple and Brāhmaṇa holdings in Kōśāṁbikāpuri situated at the confluence of the
Brahmakuṇḍi, 12 yōjanas to the east of Śrīśaila. Further, it records the gift of some land at
Kanupaṟṟu by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Vira-Ālamandala Nūṁkayadēva Chōda-mahārāja, for the
merit of Vīra-Dēvarāya-mahārāja, his queen Dēmā-ammaṅgāru and the crown prince Vijaya
Bukkarāya. Published; NDI., Vol. II, No. 55 & 56.

inscription.head:
No. 237.
(A.R. No. B 278 of 1953.)
inscription.p: This is the Telugu version of the above No. 236.

inscription.head:
No. 238.
(A.R. No. B 279 of 1953.)
Kanuparti, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District.
inscription.p: On a sculpture in front of the Chauḍamma temple.
Reads: Śrīmada[vu]ṇḍu. In characters of about the 10th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 239.
(A.R. No. B 280 of 1953.)
Mallavaram, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1247 (Śaila-vārdhi-dyumaṇi) Āśvin, Thursday, lunar eclipse.
Irregular.

Gives the genealogy of the Reḍḍi kings of Koṇḍavīḍu and records the gift of a village
to a brāhmaṇa (name lost) by the King Vēma.

l.te: bhūpā nirantan vahāti sakala pṛthvin vē-
mat kōmaṭi vēmabhūpati rasauvīraḥ kadaṁ vā-

inscription.head:
No. 240.
(A.R. No. B 281 of 1953.)
Tenāli, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District.
inscription.p:
Door jamb of the northern entrance to the maṇḍapa in front of the Rāmēśvara
temple.

Records the construction of northern entrance to the maṇḍapa of (the temple of)
Rāmēśvara of Tenāli by Narasiṁha and Nāma, the sons of Sōmaya-mantri. In characters of
the 14th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 241.
(A.R. No. B 282 of 1953.)
Tenāli, Ongole Taluk, Guntur District.
inscription.p:
Door jamb (opposite No. 239) of the northern entrance to the maṇḍapa in front of the
Rāmēśvara temple.
Records the construction of maṇṭapa-dvāra by Śiṅgaya.

inscription.head:
No. 242.
(A.R. No. B 285 of 1953.)
Bangānapalle, Banganapalle Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Pillar built into the gateway of the fort.

This is dated Śaka 1533, Sādhāraṇa, Jyēśṭha ba. 11 corresponding to 1611 A.D., May
28, Tuesday. The cyclic year was Virōdhikṛit.

Records a grant of the village Babatuturipāḍu situated in Banagānapalle-sīma by
Bhāvarāju, son of Timmarājayya and grandson of Pōchirāju Avubhalarājayyadēva-mahārāja to
the god Chennakēsavaperumāḷ of Banagānapalle. The donor is said to have obtained the
Banagānapalle-sīma as amaranāyaṅkara from the king.

l.te: ṇa saṁvvatsara jēṣṭa ba 11 lu śrīma-

inscription.head:
No. 243.
(A.R. No. B 286 of 1953.)
Anugoṇḍa, Kurnool Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Stone in basaru-kaṭṭa.

This is dated Śaka 1483, Durmati, Āshāḍha śu. 11 corresponding to 1561 A.D., June
23, Monday.

Damaged. Seems to record a gift of land to an Ārādhya (Brāhmaṇa) by Kōnēṭrājayyadēva-
mahārāja, son of Peṅnamarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 244.
(A.R. No. B 289 of 1953.)
Nāyakallu, Kurnool Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Elephant pillar in front of the Balusua Mallēśvara temple.

Damaged. Contains the Praśasti of a Mahāsāmanta who had the title Nanniveḍaṅga
and is stated to have had the boar as the lāñchchana.

inscription.head:
No. 245.
(A.R. No. B 291 of 1953.)
Nāyakallu, Kurnool Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Pillar in the maṇḍapa of Mādhavasvāmin temple.

This is dated Śaka 1470, Kīlaka, Kārttika śu. 12, corresponding to 1548 A.D., October
14, Sunday.

Records the gift of lands in Kandanavrōlu, surnamed as Tippasamudram for food
offerings to the god Mādhavarāya by Goṇḍrājulayya, son of Gauḍrāju Penamarāju of
Kāśyapa-gōtra, Āpastaṁba-sūtra. And also records the grant of lands and the gāḍibhāvi in the
village Anugoṇḍa (as the fief of Veṅkatādurāju) to the brāhmaṇas. Also refers to Śrīraṅgāchāryulu
as the guru of the donor.

valunnu sarvōtpatti samasta baḷa sahitaṁgānu dhāra-

inscription.head:
No. 246.
(A.R. No. B 292 of 1953.)
Dūdekoṇḍa, Pattikoṇḍa Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Boulder near the road by the side of the village.
This is dated Śaka 14[77], Rākshasa, Āshāḍha ba. • • • • .
Damaged. Seems to refer to a gift of an agrahara.

l.te: śubhamastu | svasti śrī jayābhyudaya śālivāhana śakavaruṣaṁbulu 14[77]

inscription.head:
No. 247.
(A.R. No. B 293 of 1953.)
Kārumañchi, Pattikonda Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Stone set up near the Chāvaḍi opposite the Bhōgēśvara temple.

Records that the stone is the Sthaḷakaṁbham (boundary pillar) of Kārumañchi, the
agrahāra of Kandāḷa Śrīraṅgāchārlu.

inscription.head:
No. 248.
(A.R. No. B 296 of 1953.)
Poṭḷapādu, Pattikoṇḍa Taluk, Kurnool District.
inscription.p: Boulder called Kālubaṇḍa outside the village.

This is dated Śaka 1446, Tāraṇa, Vaiśākha śu. 12 corresponding to 1524 A.D.,
April 15, Friday.

Incomplete. Records a gift of land made by Ayadēvamahārāja, son of Āravīṭi
Bukkarāju Timmarāju to the god Chennakēśava-perumāḷ.

inscription.head:
No. 249.
(A.R. No. B 321 of 1953.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Kūrattaḷvār shrine, west wall, proper left.

This is dated Śaka 1430, Vibhava, Jyēshṭha śu. Pañchamī, Friday, corresponding to
1508 A.D., May 5, Friday. 41.

Damaged. Seems to record the purchase and grant of land by Śrīraṅgarāja, son of
Kuchēṭi Vōbuḷarāju of Kāśyapa-gōtra, Āpastamba-sūtra and Yajuś-śakhā, for offerings to
Kūrattāḻvār.

inscription.head:
No. 250.
(A.R. No. B 322 of 1953.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Kūrattaḷvār shrine, west wall, proper left.
This is dated Śaka [1479], Pingala, Pushya śu. 12, Monday. Irregular.

Damaged. Seems to record an endowment of a sum of 2,000 chakra-varāhas, probably
towards the purchase of a village, and another, of 1,000 chakra-varāhas in the year Kāḷayukti
by Śrīraṅgarāja, son of Pōchirāja of Ātrēya-gōtra, Āpastamba-sūtra and Yajūś-śākhā for the
worship of Kūrattāḷvār and conducting the tirunakshatra (natal asterism) and the tiruvadhyayana
festivals of Bhaṭṭar. Published: SII. Volume XXIV, No. 481.

inscription.head:
No. 251.
(A.R. No. B 323 of 1953.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Kūrattaḷvār shrine, west wall, proper left.
This is dated Śaka 1478, Rākshasa, Chaitra dvādasi • • • .
Damaged. Refers to an endowment of money. The details of the gift are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 252.
(A.R. No. B 324 of 1953.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Kūrattaḷvār shrine, west wall, proper left.

This is dated Śaka 1474, Parīdhāvi, Śrāvaṇa ba. Śrījayaṁti corresponding to 1552
A.D. August 12, Friday, 78, f.d.n. 21.

Damaged. Seems to refer to an endowment of money made by Kuruchēṭi Śrīraṅgarāja
to the shrine of Kūrattāḻvār.

l.te: ·agunēṭi paridhāvi saṁvatsara śrāvaṇamāsaṁ kriṣṇapakṣaṁ śrījayaṁtināṁḍu śrīkurucēṁṭi śrīraṁgarājuluṁ-

edition.p: On a fragment next to the above inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 253.
(A.R. No. B 325 of 1953.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Kūrattaḷvār shrine, Tiers of the west wall.

This is dated Śaka 1480, Piṅgala, Āśvīja ba. 10, Sunday corresponding to 1557
A.D., October 17. The Śaka year quoted is current.

Records a gift of land by Ēkāṅgi Naraśiṅgayya, a devotee of Bhaṭṭar (successor of
Rāmānuja), for the feeding of the Śrī Vaishṇavas. Published; S.I.I., Vol. XXIV, No. 480.

l.te: śrīmatē rāmānujāyanamaḥ || svasti śrī vijayābhyudaya śālivāhana śakava¨rṣaṁbulu

inscription.head:
No. 254.
(A.R. No. B 326 of 1953.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Kūrattaḷvār shrine, Tiers of the south wall.

This is dated Śaka year 1531, Kīlaka, Chaitra śu. 2, Monday corresponding 1608
A.D. March 7, f.d.t. °23, Rēvatī, 91. The Śaka year quoted is current.

Records an endowment of 60 varāhas and another of 100 varāhas for worship and
offerings of curd-rice on the days of the natal stars of Periyāḻvār and others. Published: S.I.I.,
Vol. XXIV. No. 519.

inscription.head:
No. 255.
(A.R. No. B 332 of 1953.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Kūrattaḷvār shrine, pillar in the mukha-maṇḍapa.
This is dated Śaka 1803, other details are lost.

Fragmentary and damaged. Refers to some provision made for worship in the temple
of Kūrattu Āḷvār. Published: S.I.I., Vol. XXIV, No. 639.

inscription.head:
No. 256.
(A.R. No. B 334 of 1953.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: Gold anklet of Śrī-Raṅganāthasvāmin (mūla-vigraha).

This is dated Śaka 1656, Ānaṁda, Māgha śu, 5. Friday corresponding to 1735 A.D.,
January 17, f.d.t. 23. The nakshatra was Uttarabhādrapada f.t.n. 03.

Records that a pair of gold anklets with tinkling bells (gejjalu) is the offering of
Pedda-veṅkaṭaperumāḷ-nāyanivāru, son of Lakshmīdēvammavāru, the queen of Tupākula
Rāmaliṁgayya-nāyanivāru. The weight of the set is given as 468(1/2) varāhas. Below this is
engraved a later record stating that the anklets were re-made with an additional gold of the
weight of 40 rupees by the munificence of Javīra Bāyi Bhagavaṇ Dās, mother of G.B.
Narōttamdās of Bombay, in the month of September 1926, and that the weight of the jewel
after remaking is 183 tolas. Published: S.I.I., Vol. XXIV, No. 568.

inscription.head:
No. 257.
(A.R. No. B 345 of 1953.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: On a gold Kalaśa.

States that this is the offering of Vijayaraṅga-chokkanāthanāyaka to god Kastūri
Raṅganāyaka. Published: S.I.I., Vol. XXIV, No. 571.

l.te: śrī kastūri raṁganāyakuluku śrī vijayaraṁga cokkanāthayya vubha

inscription.head:
No. 258.
(A.R. No. B 346 of 1953.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: On a gold bowl with a stand.

States that this is an offering of Vijayaraṅga Chōkkanātha-nāyaka. Published: S.I.I., Vol.
XXIV, No. 572.

inscription.head:
No. 259.
(A.R. No. B 348 of 1953.)
Śrīraṅgam, Tiruchinapalli Taluk and District, Tamilnadu.
inscription.p: On a golden palanquin.

This is dated Śaka 1735, Śrīmukha, Kārttika śu 11, Bhṛiguvāra corresponding to
1813 A.D., November 4, Thursday (not Friday).

States that Tōḷikiniyāṉ (the palanquin), which was the offering of Vijayaraṅga-
chokkanāthanāyaka to the god Raṅganātha, having become dilapidated was repaired by Mr.
George Francis Trevers, the collector. The weight of gold used is 39(3/4) seers and that of
silver 70(1/2) seers. Published: S.I.I., Vol. XXIV, No. 641.
